All About Katameya Heights New Cairo Compound
=============================================

--- CONTENT ---
Katameya Heights New Cairo
Katameya Heights New Cairo​ is well-known as a high-end, luxurious compound developed by the reputable real estate developer, Starlight Developments. Since the amenities at this compound are one-of-a-kind, this article is dedicated to spotlighting them by providing a full account of the project’s facilities and master plan.
So, are you ready to be amazed at what Katameya Heights New Cairo​ holds for you?
Let’s get started.
Katameya Heights New Cairo​​ Amenities
Katameya Heights New Cairo​ spans a land area of 1.5 million square meters in
New Cairo
. The creative concept of turning the dry sand into lush, rolling hills led to the establishment of Katameya Heights Golf and Tennis Resort, Egypt’s first commercial venture to combine golf and real estate.
Cairo’s corporate landscape has been influenced by Katameya Heights’ spectacular clubhouse, finely designed golf course, and luxurious homes.
Starlight Developments
developed the compound to boast a wide range of facilities. Those include the premium clubhouse, the kids center, the health club, the nursery, the 18-hole golf course, and various dining options.
In fact, each and every point of these can be written in articles, not just a single one, but to give you the best of both worlds, the center of all the attention,  we’ll be talking about the clubhouse mainly in this article.
Katameya Heights New Cairo Amenities
Explore Katameya Heights New Cairo properties
Katameya Heights New Cairo​ Elite Clubhouse
The Katameya Heights New Cairo Clubhouse is a one-of-a-kind, filled to the brim with premium amenities created specifically to make you enjoy your time and have great quality time with your loved ones.
The building is a genuinely magnificent structure situated on a significant portion of the golf course’s exquisitely planned landscape. Activity in the clubhouse is vibrant every day and into the evening. The building is filled with a variety of restaurants, pubs, lounges, and patios with varying settings and tones, the
There are numerous entertainment options in the clubhouse to further enhance the harmony. Particularly with the tournaments organized throughout the year, play areas create a sense of fun and fulfill the demand for some recreational challenges.
The clubhouse features the Golf Pro Shop, the health club, the various dining venues, the classic lounge, the pool, the terrace, the trophy room, the bridge room, and finally the Calcutta bar.
Let us talk about each of them in a bit of detail in the following sections.
Katameya Heights New Cairo Amenities
Golf Pro Shop of Katameya Heights Clubhouse
In the clubhouse, you will find The Golf Pro Shop.  When you’re choosing, a good assortment of golf memorabilia could be confusing. For this reason, knowledgeable staff members are available to help and provide tailored guidance when you buy golf equipment.
Brand-name accessories, equipment, caps, towels, apparel with logos, and a variety of high-end mementos. There is a sizable fleet of golf carts to assist the golfers near the clubhouse entrance and the pro shop.
The Dining Venues in Katameya Heights Compound​ Clubhouse
Also in the clubhouse, you will find the awesome dining venues and restaurants. A range of dining options, bars, and lounges can be found in the clubhouse, which has a view of the pool. The Swing Restaurant and Lounge is elegant yet incredibly comfortable.
Enjoy a little sunshine inside the restaurant while admiring the serene pool water view, or receive even more of it at the Swing terrace with its unique cool breeze. The Mulligan Pub and Sushi Bar serves as a gathering place for two very distinct types of junkies: sushi and golf addicts.
Dining Venues in Katameya Heights
The Katameya Heights New Cairo​ Health Club
The clubhouse also features a health club by the pool. The health club blends calm and vibrancy in one location. The thrilling gym and the charming spa are next to each other, and both are conveniently located beside the pool for a full session of physical therapy. There are detoxifying saunas, a stimulating steam room, and sensory-pleasing Jacuzzis at the spa.
The very talented massage therapist offers a range of massage treatments. The gym next door is outfitted with Nautilus equipment for a variety of fitness exercises. Here, too, expert trainers serve as mentors. Four months a year, the pool outside the health club is heated and has an in-water bar.
The Classic Lounge of the Katameya Heights  Clubhouse
On the second floor of the club, you will find the classic lounge. The Classic Lounge creates a cozy, non-monotonous ambiance thanks to the seats of various materials and styles positioned in various ways around the large space. You can sit at the window, on the leather set, or by the fireplace.
When it’s quiet, the Classic Lounge is a great place to read or have a private conversation. The lounge can accommodate up to 150 guests and is very spacious, making it ideal for private events.
Katameya Heights Lounge
The Katameya Heights New Cairo​ Clubhouse Terrace
You will be able to see a wonderful vista as soon as you step outside. A large portion of the golf course’s beauty is seen from the terrace, which is located on the second floor of the clubhouse.
With a view of the pool from one side and a stunning waterfall from the other, the tables are arranged across the large area. Up to 1000 guests can be accommodated in the expanding space, which is ideal for weddings and other gatherings.
The Bridge Room of the Katameya Heights​ Clubhouse
On the second floor of the clubhouse, beside the classic lounge and the terrace, you will find the bridge room. The traditional spirit of bridge, involving skill and the excitement of chance, is undoubtedly revived in this hall.
The space has a collection of green felt tables with comfortable chairs in the style of a casino. Members of the clubhouse and bridge enthusiasts frequent the bridge room at Katameya Heights. Events that are held are typically exciting and a lot of fun.
Trophy Lounge of Katameya Heights New Cairo​ Clubhouse
Another peaceful location for private conversations and leisure time is reserved for VIPs. In addition to being elegant, the Trophy Lounge is intriguing.
The interior design, the colors of the furnishings, and the soft lighting all combine to create a celebrity atmosphere. In addition to its elegance, it has enough space to accommodate a wedding or celebration for up to 100 people.
The Calcutta Bar of Katameya Heights ​ Clubhouse
The Calcutta Bar of Katameya Heights New Cairo, which dates back to the 18th century, is situated in the middle of the clubhouse’s second floor. Quite a few pool players, bridge enthusiasts, and avid golfers congregate there.
Observing the décor, bar finishes, and the outstanding bartenders offering you some of your favorite drinks with a twist will make you feel as though you are in the classical era.
Now What?
Now that you know all about Katameya Heights New Cairo​ and its clubhouse, it is high time you took action.

Siwa Egypt Exquisite Vacations & Beautiful Destinations

--- CONTENT ---
Siwa Egypt
When El Sahel Season is over, winter comes with its charm and grace. It also gets accompanied by seasonal depression – especially if you are a person who enjoys a short escapade every once in a while. But coastal vacations aren’t the only gateway from the city! Egypt exhibits boundless destinations, serenity, and adventure seekers should put on their to-do list, and one of them is the beautiful Siwa Oasis.
When in Egypt, especially during the cold weather, you have to explore the wonders of Siwa, unravel its authentic vibes, and enjoy its Bedouin culture. This article will take you on a tour of its distinguishing features that will give you a blast!
Siwa Oasis in Egypt
Egypt is a magical tourist destination, with its unbounded allure and exquisite destinations. The North Coast is the locals’ favorite summer escapade, but when winter arrives, there are fascinating havens where they can ditch the hassle and bustle of the city. And one of them lies in the Western Desert.
Siwa Oasis is one of the most enchanting destinations in Egypt, around 740 km away from Cairo. It is not only tranquil but also somewhere your vacation can be rejuvenating as well as adventurous. From various sightseeing attractions to vast adrenaline-booster activities, are you ready to create unforgettable memories and revive your mind and body?
Siwa Oasis in Egypt is a dazzling haven, with a myriad of activities for those seeking:
Relaxation and revival
An endorphin rush
Taking a glimpse at what comprises the Bedouin culture of Siwa

places to go in Egypt during winter

Shali Fortress in Siwa Egypt
Where to Wander in Siwa?
Siwa Oasis has its authentic charm, and it is comprised of boundless sightseeing destinations where you can learn about the area’s culture and history, including:
The Shali Fortress: which holds the ingenuity and strength of the Siwan people throughout its land. It is made of mud-brick, salt rock, and many natural materials that give the Shali Fortress its natural glamour.
Mountain of the Dead: one of Siwa’s archeological sites. It has thousands of graves and it dates back to around the 3rd century BC.
Temple of the Oracle of Amun / Aghurmi: which was built in the 6th century BC. It was dedicated to Amun. It is considered a powerful symbol of the town’s prosperity.
The Traditional Siwa House: made of mud and wood displays,. It depicts the historicity of Siwa Oasis and its enchanting spirits.
Graeco-Roman Tombs: combining the Greek and Roman architectural styles in building their deceased tombs.
Cleopatra’s Pool – Spring of Juba: where you can immerse yourself in its warm, mineral-rich water.
Temple of Umm Ubayd: that was dedicated to Amun. It is a large white land that was built more than 2 thousand years ago.

Explore The Top Things to do in Alexandria Egypt
================================================

--- CONTENT ---
Top Things to do in Alexandria
Alexandria Egypt, is one of the most beautiful coastal cities. Many people want to visit the city and explore its beauty but they lack a plan; they don’t know what they will be doing there. This is why this blog is here, to tell you all about the top things to do in Alexandria Egypt.
Whether you are a shopping lover, a history admirer, or a food guru, Alexandria, Egypt, offers many options for having fun and enjoying a great time.
In this article, we will tell you all about Alexandria Egypt, its treasures, and the top things to do in Alexandria.
So, are you ready?
Top Things To Do in Alexandria Egypt
Things to do in Alexandria
Alexandria Egypt is full of hidden gems. Thanks to its being Egypt’s largest metropolis, one of the largest towns in the Mediterranean region, and a hub of Hellenic learning and science, Alexandria, one of Egypt’s biggest cities, also serves as both a major industrial hub and the country’s main harbor.
The city is now a major one with many activities to be done within its borders, thanks to all these features.
Among the things to do in Alexandria, you will find the  following:
Sightseeing
Shopping
Dining
Fun activities
We will break down each of these in detail in the following sections.
Sightseeing in Alexandria Egypt
Among the first-rate things to do in Alexandria, Egypt, is sightseeing. Thanks to its historical city status, Alexandria boasts a wide range of ancient sights and monuments.
First, you can visit the ancient archaeological site called the Catacombs of Kom El Shoqafa (Mound of Shards). It was regarded as one of the Seven Wonders in the Middle Ages. With a blend of Roman, Greek, and Egyptian architectural elements, the catacombs were first utilized as a cemetery for city dwellers in the second century AD.
The Qaitbay Citadel is a fortified fortification from the 15th century that is situated near Alexandria, Egypt, on the Mediterranean coast. In 1477 AD, Sultan Al-Ashraf Sayf al-Din Qa’it Bay founded it. The Citadel is located near the mouth of the Eastern Harbor on the eastern side of Pharos Island’s northern tip.
Also, you cannot miss visiting Alexandria, Egypt’s Bibliotheca Alexandrina, also known as the Library of Alexandria, is a significant library and cultural hub on the Mediterranean coast. The front features hieroglyphs from ancient Egypt.
Alexandria, Egypt’s Bibliotheca Alexandrina, is a significant library and cultural hub situated on the Mediterranean Sea’s shore. It is an attempt to revive some of the genius that this former hub of learning and scholarship embodied, as well as a memorial to the Library of Alexandria that was lost to time.
Things-to-do-in-Alexandria -Library
Sightseeing Sights Continued
Alexandria, Egypt’s Montaza Public Park is home to the Palestine Hotel and the Royal Palace. Montaza Palace is surrounded by a sizable park called Montaza Park. Beautiful flowers, strolling trails, and breathtaking views of the Mediterranean Sea can all be found in the park.
Both residents and visitors wishing to get away from the bustle of the city frequently visit this well-liked location. A picnic in the park is a terrific idea.
You can also visit Sidi Abu Al Abbas Al Mursi Mosque to see its historical and artistic architecture. There are entrances to the mosque in the east and north, both of which look out over the square.
The mosque’s central section is an octagon with interior walls. With the exception of a 5.6-meter-tall mosaic, they were clothed in artificial stone. Arabesque decorations adorn the lofty ceiling. A Shokhsheikha, a large octagonal skylight, is located within.
Abu Al Abbas Al Mursi Mosque Alexandria
Museums in Alexandria Egypt
First of all, you can go visit Montaza Palace which is a public museum dedicated to the family history of the Muhammad Ali Dynasty. The final khedive of Egypt and Sudan, Khedive Abbas II, constructed it in 1892. The palace is well-liked by both locals and visitors since it is encircled by beautiful gardens and has a view of the Mediterranean Sea.
Moreover, the renovated Italian-style mansion in Alexandria, Egypt, is home to the Alexandria National Museum. About 1,800 objects from the Pharaonic, Roman, Coptic, and Islamic periods are among the many that tell the tale of Alexandria’s history.
You can also visit the home where the renowned Greek poet Constantine P. Cavafy spent twenty-five years living and working, which is now known as the Cavafy Museum. First editions of his writings, manuscripts, and personal items are also on display at the museum.
Visiting Alexandria’s little Odeum, a Roman amphitheater, is also a great idea. Gladiatorial fights and other public performances took place in the well-preserved Roman Amphitheater in Alexandria. It is currently accessible to the public as a tourist destination after being founded in 1960, when the building was underway.
Consider visiting the Graeco-Roman Museum was first constructed in 1892 as a modest structure on Horreya Road. Actually, it moved to its current location close to Gamal Abdul Nasser Road in 1895.
Additionally, it had eleven galleries when it first opened and expanded during subsequent refurbishment phases. In 1984, the 25th gallery was opened. Indeed, a wide range of coins from various nations may be found at the Greco-Roman Museum in Alexandria.
Greco-Roman Museum
Alexandria Museums Continued
The Qaitbay Maritime Museum in Alexandria features a variety of marine life and landscapes. They gathered from the Mediterranean and Red Seas. Natural coral reefs can be found at the Qaitbay Maritime Museum in Alexandria.
It also has several seashells and a variety of fish, including the Picasso fish. A collection of skates and rays is the next exhibit at the Qaitbay Maritime Museum in Alexandria.
Consider also visiting The Royal Jewelry Museum. Established by Muhammad Ali Pasha in 1805, the Egyptian royal family ruled for 150 years until 1952, and their valuables are on exhibit in the Royal Jewelry Museum.
The palace in Zizinya, Alexandria, was constructed in 1919 by Zaynab Fahmi. Fatma al-Zahra’, the daughter of Prince Ali Haider, a descendant of Muhammad Ali Pasha, lived there.
The palace provides a dramatic setting for the family’s beautiful belongings. Its distinctive fusion of European and Islamic design reflects the royal family’s exquisite taste, as evidenced by the palace rooms’ mosaics, gilded ceilings, and paintings.
Things to do in Alexandria – Royal Jewelry Museum
The places you can visit and things to do in Alexandria are limitless, but, let us now proceed to the shopping experience in Alexandria.
Shopping in Alexandria Egypt
Going shopping is among the top-rated activities and things to do in Alexandria Egypt. You can go shopping in malls or in the open-air, well-known streets of Alexandria.
As for the malls, Alexandria is well-known for the following shopping malls:
City Centre Alexandria
San Stefano Grand Plaza
Green Plaza Mall
City Light Mall
Matajer Mall
Alexa Tower Shopping Mall
Majestic Mall
Town Mall
Designia Mall
You can also enjoy shopping on the very well-known “Khaled Ebn Al Walid” Street. On this street, you will find many shops and boutique stores specializing in different types of goods to cater to all the needs of both; the residents of the city and the visitors looking for souvenirs.
Now, after tackling the top things to do in Alexandria from sightseeing and shopping, we can proceed to talk about the unique dining experience offered in Alexandria Egypt.
City Centre Alexandria
Dining Experience in Alexandria Egypt
The dining experience in Alexandria takes the third level in the hierarchy of the top things to do in Alexandria Egypt.
Being a medium for many cultures, Alexandria offers a rich culinary experience featuring Greek, Italian, and Egyptian cuisines. You will find the seafood and the street-truck food.
First and foremost, being a coastal city, you cannot miss eating seafood in Alexandria. You can choose your food from the following well-known options:
Halaket El Smk – Fish Market, and get your food cooked, or you can just go to one of the high-end seafood restaurants in Alexandria
Dairy products and ice cream stores such as “Alban Swisra”, “Azza”, and “Makram”
“Kebdt El Falah” street food store that is always crowded
By now, we have covered all about the top things to do in Alexandria Egypt. But, we cannot end our article without talking about the city itself so, let us know about it in the following section.
Don’t forget to check
Top Things To Do in North Coast
All About Alexandria Egypt
We cannot be talking about the things to do in
Alexandria
without talking about the city itself.
Known as the Mermaid of the Mediterranean, Alexandria has long held this title. Alexandria was Egypt’s sole capital in the past, but now it is considered the country’s second capital and its second-largest city after Cairo.
The Alexandria city which is named after Alexander the Great, was constructed in 331 B.C. to serve as the Hellenic-Roman Capital of Egypt and a global hub of culture and civilization. It is located on the Mediterranean Sea.
Alexandria is now a popular tourist destination and year-round beach resort with historical attractions for those who want to see ancient monuments, get a taste of Alexandria’s illustrious past, or just take advantage of the sun, sea breeze, golden sandy beaches, and exciting entertainment venues.
Numerous museums, monuments, and artifacts from the Pharaonic, Hellenic, Roman, Coptic, and Islamic eras attest to Alexandria’s history as a crossroads of cultures and civilizations since its founding by Alexander the Great.
Alexandria has been a staple on the travel itinerary of visitors to Egypt because of its exceptional location with a view of the
Mediterranean
, its pleasant climate, its numerous things to do, and its lovely beaches.
In order to boost the yacht tourism business, it was essential to construct a marina for yachts. Additionally, hosting local and international boat racing contests increased the city’s attraction to visitors from all over the world.
Finally,  Alexandria is located on a 70-kilometer stretch of coastline to the northwest of the Delta. The Mediterranean Sea borders the governorate on the north, Behera borders it on the east, and the Matrouh governorate borders it on the west.

Alexandria Compounds
from this article.

Full guide to Sixth Settlement Compounds, Location & More
=========================================================

--- CONTENT ---
Sixth Settlement
Sixth Settlement is one of the recent areas in New Cairo City. It is created with upscale amenities, enjoys a strategic location, and is considered the new extension of the city.
Now you are probably wondering what is the location and compounds of the Sixth Settlement, New Cairo. Then you need to read this article to the very end, as it will give you all the necessary details about the area!
Sixth Settlement in New Cairo
Formerly known as “Al Aml Triangle”, the Sixth Settlement in New Cairo is now one of the most trending areas, especially with its upscale projects.
In recent news, The Ministry of Housing, Utilities, and Urban Communities has announced the official launch of Sixth Settlement in New Cairo over a land area of 4800 acres (20 million square meters).
View this post on Instagram
A post shared by Nawy (@nawyrealestate)
We cannot talk about the Sixth Settlement of New Cairo without mentioning its location, so, let’s tell you more about the location map of the Sixth Settlement in the next section.
Sixth Settlement Location Map
Sixth Settlement is an extension of
New Cairo
City and this is why its location connects the city with other major cities.
It is planned that South 90th Street will be the major road and axis passing by the district and linking it with the rest of the settlements.
Bear in mind that the Sixth Settlement is placed at the end point of the Fifth Settlement, in between Ain Sokhna Road, and Middle Ring Road, and very close to the
New Administrative Capital
.
Now that you know the overview of the Sixth Settlement and its location in New Cairo, let us tell you more about the projects in the area.
Sixth Settlement
Top Projects in Sixth Settlement
6th Settlement area includes one of the best investment hubs which is “Beit Al Watan”.This Beit Al Watan project is developed by the New Urban Communities Authority.
It rolls over a land area of 240 acres in the Sixth Settlement and it offers many housing and investment opportunities for the Egyptians abroad.

ZED East by Ora Developers

R7 or R8 at New Capital? Check Compounds, Location & Photos
===========================================================

--- CONTENT ---
R7 or R8 at New Capital
If you’ve been eyeing Egypt’s New cities and asking which districts are making waves, then you’ve probably come across R7 or R8 at New Capital. Both districts are home to some of the most luxurious compounds, offering a modern and elevated lifestyle.
But what exactly are R7 and R8? How do they differ from one another, and what are the standout projects in each? Where should you purchase your home?
In this blog, we’ll break down everything you need to know about these two premier districts, highlighting the key differences, top projects, and what makes each neighborhood unique.
R7 or R8 at New Capital: A Quick Overview
New Administrative Capital is rapidly becoming one of the most exciting and innovative urban developments in the country (
In the Middle East, to be honest!
).
Designed to ease congestion in Cairo, it’s set to become a hub for business, government, and modern living. Among its many districts, R7 and R8 stand out as two of the most coveted areas filled with top-notch commercial and residential projects.
But what makes them unique, and what are the key differences?
Before diving into the nitty-gritty information, let’s take a quick look at New Capital City itself.
It’s a futuristic urban project with state-of-the-art infrastructure, green spaces, and smart city technologies, offering a modern living experience.
New Capital City is located 35 kilometers east of Cairo, between two main roads: The Cairo – Suez and Cairo – Ain Sokhna Roads.
This is part of a 40,000-acre first phase within the 170-acre area of the New Administrative Capital.
Explore Top Projects in New Capital
After knowing some things about the New Capital City of Egypt, the main question arises:
What are the differences between R7 and R8?
That’s what we’ll be touching on below.
Comparison Between R7 And R8: Key Differences
Now you are surely asking yourself, “R7 or R8 at New Capital, which District is right for me?” Both are awesome, so how should I choose the best zone to locate my new home?
In a simple way
:
Location
R7 New Capital connects all the services available in the New Administrative Capital, making it the closest to the regional ring road and the Al Masa Hotel, as well as being near Expo City.
In contrast, the R8 area is distinguished by its elevated land, which is higher than the surrounding neighborhoods, and it is the closest to the diplomatic district.
Developments Foucues
Many people view R7 as a more affordable and family-oriented residential area, making it appealing to middle-income families and individuals.
On the other hand, R8 is more focused on luxurious mixed-use projects and green spaces. The area is designed to offer a more serene environment while still offering excellent services and amenities.
Total land Area
:
R7 New Capital covers 1500 acres of land
Meanwhile, R8 District has a total land area of 2500 acres of land
R7 VS R8
In summary, R7 is more central with a focus on mixed-use development, business, and accessibility, while R8 leans more toward luxury, privacy, and a peaceful atmosphere.
Both are highly desirable, but your choice would depend on whether you prefer being closer to the action or living in a more tranquil, green space.
R7 Top Compounds
R7 New Capital District is primarily focused on high-end residential compounds, and it offers a peaceful and exclusive living experience.

is one of the most sought-after and luxurious compounds in R7. The project was developed by the real estate giant, Misrt Italia, across an impressive area of 110 acres of land.
Vinci
From the commercial to the residential units for sale, Vinci New Capital has a wide array of properties for sale at the best prices and exclusive payment plans.

Discover Hacienda Blue Prices (Install Over 10 Years)

--- CONTENT ---
Hacienda Blue Prices
Hacienda Blue is one of the recent projects by Palm Hills Developments and it comes as one of the most mesmerizing North Coast resorts. Driven by its name, the resort guarantees the domination of the blue color of the beach throughout the entire project which made it an attractive summer spot. This is exactly why this blog will give you all the details about Hacienda Blue prices and more.
Hacienda Blue Prices
Hacienda Blue features many upscale summer experiences and opulent properties for sale and thus its prices are set accordingly.
The upscale collection of properties available for sale at Hacienda Blue include:
standalone villas
townhouses
chalets
cabins
Accordingly, Hacienda Blue prices start at 18,000,000 EGP and go up to 113,000,000 EGP based on the property type, size, and location you choose.
Hacienda Blue Prices
We will thoroughly explore the details of Hacienda Blue units and prices in the following sections.
Villas For Sale in Hacienda Blue
The standalone villas in Hacienda Blue North Coast come in three different styles as in the following:
1st row from the beach standalone villa
2nd row from the beach standalone villa
Water villa overlooking the lagoon
The prices for such villas vary according to their location, size, and number of bedrooms as follows:
1st row from the beach standalone villas with 6 master bedrooms have a price range between 110,000,000 EGP and 113,000,000 EGP.
2nd row from the beach standalone villas with 5 master bedrooms have an average price of 73,000,000 EGP.
Water villas overlooking a 24-acre lagoon, come featuring 5 master bedrooms and have a starting price of 58,500,000 EGP.
Townhouses in Hacienda Blue North Coast For Sale
Townhouses in Hacienda Blue are called “Water townhouses” as they come overlooking a lagoon of 24 acres.
These water townhouses feature 4 master bedrooms and can be middle or corner and their prices are as follows:
middle townhouses start at 30,000,000 EGP
corner townhouses start at 32,000,000 EGP
Hacienda Blue Chalets
Concerning the chalets in Hacienda Blue, they come in two categories; junior and senior. The price ranges vary accordingly as follows:
senior 1st-floor chalet with 4 master bedrooms, starting at 24,000,000 EGP.
senior ground floor chalets have a starting price of 26,500,000 EGP.
junior ground floor chalets with 3 master bedrooms start at 19,000,000 EGP.
junior 1st-floor chalets with 3 master bedrooms + nanny room start at 21,500,000 EGP.
Cabins for Sale in Hacienda Blue
Cabins in Hacienda Blue have two types:
beach cabin
lagoon cabin (single or double)
However, if you want a lagoon cabin (overlooking the lagoon), then you need to take a villa or a townhouse but, that is not the case for the beach cabins.
Concerning their prices, they are as follows:
beach cabins come fully-finished and have a price range between 47,500,000 EGP and 49,000,000 EGP
single lagoon cabins have a price range between 18,000,000 EGP and 19,000,000 EGP
double lagoon cabins, on the other hand, come in a price range between 34,000,000 EGP and 36,000,000 EGP
Payment Plans
Palm Hills Development
offers flexible payment plans for Hacienda Blue.
You will pay a 5% down payment, another 5% after 3 months then monthly escalating installments for 10 years.
Please note that the prices and spaces of the available units are constantly updated.
For the latest update of the price list, please check out the
Hacienda Blue
listings.
Also, don’t forget that Palm Hills has many other upscale resorts in the North Coast which include:
Hacienda West
Hacienda Waters
Hacienda Bay
Hacienda Heneish
Hacienda White
Taking Action

Also, it is worth noting that thanks to being in a premium location on the 165th km of International Coastal Road, opposite Dabaa Road, and being only 28 kilos from Alamein Airport, Hacienda Blue enjoys a strategic location in Ras El Hekma, North Coast.
Don’t forget that Hacienda Blue features a very clear sandy beach with a 560m beachfront and 1 km of depth alongside a 24-acre lagoon and landscaping of 60%.

Check Out The Best Private Universities in Egypt​
=================================================

--- CONTENT ---
Private Universities in Egypt
Many people prioritize the education of their children and consider it their number one goal in life. They always strive to give their little ones the best education possible. This is why, upon finishing high school, many parents and students start to look for the best private and global universities in Egypt.
For many people, the best university is the one with the top ranking; for others, it is the one within close proximity to the area where they are living.
Whether you are in this group or that, this blog is for you. It will guide you through the best private universities in Egypt with top rankings and in different residential areas.
Best Private Universities in Egypt
In fact, the list of the best private global universities in Egypt is very long, but the following are just an example:
American University in Cairo – AUC
German University in Cairo – GUC
Future University in Egypt – FUE
Misr International University – MIU
British University in Egypt – BUE
October University for Modern Sciences and Arts – MSA
Misr University for Science and Technology – MUST
Badya University
New Giza University
Nile University
In the following sections, we will talk about each of them in a bit of detail.
Best Private Universities in Egypt
You can also check the
International Schools In Egypt: New Cairo, Zayed, Alexandria
American University in Cairo – AUC
The American University in Cairo is one of the top-notch educational institutions in
New Cairo
.
This exceptional university has been one of the best American-accredited institutions of higher education in Egypt since 1919. It not only offers the students a premium educational curriculum but also the best social and cultural experience.
The
American University in Cairo
currently features 25 departments and institutes offering bachelor’s, master’s, and graduate diploma programs, 12 cross-discipline research centers,39 undergraduate majors, 52 master’s programs, and two PhD programs.
However, it is well-known that the American University in Cairo is among the top universities in Egypt for engineering.
The American University in Cairo has four main schools, each of which has various disciplines beneath, and these schools include:
Onsi Sawiris School of Business features many programs, including Business Administration in management of information and communication technology, Business and Entrepreneurship, and marketing.
School of Sciences and Engineering features Actuarial Science, Data Science, Applied Probability and Statistics, Archeological Chemistry, and Behavioral Neuroscience.
School of Global Affairs and Public Policy features Political Science and International Human Rights Law, Integrated Marketing Communication, Multimedia Communication, and Journalism.
School of Humanities and Social Sciences features Anthropology, Arabic Studies, Egyptology, English and Comparative Literature, Graphic Design, and History.
It should be noted that the fees of the AUC are calculated per credit hour, and the credit hour for Egyptian undergraduate students costs $667, which is equivalent to 32,852.50 EGP.
Top Universities – AUC
German University in Cairo – GUC
The German University in Cairo is among the best private universities in Egypt, which was established in 2002 and is located in New Cairo.
It is the first integrated German university outside Germany to offer 3 Bologna cycles, namely B.Sc., M.Sc., and PhD, featuring 71 study programs (31 undergraduate and 40 postgraduate).
The German University in Cairo features 7 faculties, including:
Faculty of Information Engineering and Technology
Faculty of Engineering and Materials Science
Faculty of Media Engineering and Technology
Faculty of Management Technology
Faculty of Applied Science and Arts
Faculty of Pharmacy and Biotechnology
Faculty of postgraduate studies and research
Lastly, the German University in Cairo has a large campus of 577000 square meters, including a 70400 square meters sports area.
The GUC varies according to the discipline; however, the range starts at 89,400 EGP  and scales up to 136,390 EGP.
Private Universities in Egypt- GUC
Future University in Egypt – FUE
Future University in Egypt (FUE) is one of the highly acclaimed private universities in Egypt.
This university started in 2006, and it currently hosts 6 faculties, including:
Faculty of Oral and Dental Medicine
Faculty of Pharmacy
Faculty of Economics and Political Science
Faculty of Commerce and Business Administration
Faculty of Engineering and Technology
Faculty of Computers and Information Technology
Prices and fees of Future University start at 161,900 EGP.
Future University
Misr International University – MIU
Misr International University is counted among the best private universities in Egypt for its long history (started in 1996) and its premium location in Oubour on Cairo – Ismaila Road.
This university features eight disciplines, including:
Alsun
Computer Science
Mass Communication
Business Administration
Oral & Dental Medicine
Pharmacy
Engineering – Architecture
Engineering – Electronics
Lastly, the university provides its students with all the possible facilities such as a music room, art room, sports facilities & fitness center, and study rooms.
The fees range for MIU start at 100,000 EGP.
Private Universities -MIU.
British University in Egypt – BUE
The British University in Egypt is one of the top-notch educational institutes in
El Shorouk City
.
This university started in 2005 on over 60 acres of land, and it has grown over time to include 11 faculties.
These faculties include:
Faculty of Renewable Energy & Environmental Engineering
Faculty of Art and Design
Faculty of Arts and Humanities
Faculty of Business Administration
Faculty of Economics and Political Science
Faculty of Communication and Mass Media
Faculty of Dentistry
Faculty of Energy and Environmental Engineering
Faculty of Engineering
Faculty of Informatics and Computer Science
Faculty of Law
Faculty of Nursing
Faculty of Pharmacy
It is one of a few fine arts universities in Egypt​ since this discipline is not available in all universities that offer the main disciplines of medicine and engineering.
Last but not least, the university is accredited by the UK Quality Assurance Agency for Higher Education being the first university in Egypt and North Africa to get this accreditation.
The annual fees of BUE are based on the student’s high school grade upon enrollment in the university; however, fees start at 62,000 EGP.
BUE
October University for Modern Sciences and Arts – MSA
October University for Modern Sciences and Arts is among the reputable educational institutions in
6 October City
.
This university has a long history dating back to 1996, which started with a 50-acre campus (40% of which is greenery).
Now, the university has more than 13 student activities, and more than 700 computer labs and art studios.
October University for Modern Sciences and Arts offers 10 disciplines, including:
Arts and Design
Biotechnology
Computer Science
Dentistry, Engineering
Languages
Mass Communication
Management Sciences
Pharmacy
Physical Therapy
MSA’s fees start at 15,000 EGP for different schools.
Universities-MSA
Misr University for Science and Technology – MUST
Misr University for Science and Technology is one of the best private universities in Egypt which was established in 1996. It is an active member of the Association of Arab Universities and the Association of African Universities.
The university features more than ten faculties and it provides its students with many high-end facilities. Those include a central library, community center, mosque, test centers, arts center, museum, F&B stations, and labs.
New Giza University schools include:
School of Medicine
School of Dentistry
School of Pharmacy
School of Business and Finance
School of Economics and Politics
School of Information Technology
School of Engineering
School of Arts and Design
Finally, Misr University for Science and Technology features many disciplines; however, it can be considered among the most sought-after computer science universities in Egypt.
MUST University fees have a range of 225,000 EGP.
MUST University
Badya University
Badya University is a high-end educational facility inside
Badya October
by Palm Hills Development.
This university was established in 2023 over a huge land area of 167,000 square meters. It is created to be a fully-fledged educational gateway within Badya City. This is why it is set to host seven different faculties, five of which are in action.
Those five include:
School of Medicine
School of Dentistry

School of Computer Science

The fees of Badya University start at 249,000 EGP.
University – Badya

New Giza Compound
by G Developments (formerly New Giza Developments).
This university is one of the best multidisciplinary private universities in Egypt, which was established in 2016.
It currently hosts 8 faculties with future plans to expand to 4 more faculties. Also, the campus is filled to the brim with many high-end student facilities. Students will find laboratories, F&B courts, and student lounges.

Top IB Schools In Egypt​ : Your Full Guide
==========================================

--- CONTENT ---
IB Schools in Egypt
Elite education is the top priority of parents looking for a new school for their little ones. That is why all parents start to learn about and understand the differences between various educational systems, such as the local systems and international ones, including the American, the British, the Deutsch, and the International Baccalaureate (IB) ones. However, this article will focus on IB schools in Egypt.
In this article, you will learn about the top IB schools in Egypt, including their rankings, locations, and the curricula they offer. Also, you will get to learn all about the International Baccalaureate (IB) system and its perks.
So, just keep reading this article till its very end to grasp all the information about these schools.

IB Schools in Egypt
Schools in Egypt started to adopt the International Baccalaureate system in 1989. There are 53 IB schools in Egypt, mainly in Cairo, Giza, Alexandria, and the Red Sea. Here is a list of the most prominent IB schools in Egypt:

IB-Schools-In-Egypt
Don’t forget to
check out the best private universities in Egypt
!
What are International Baccalaureate (IB) Schools?
International Baccalaureate (IB) schools in Egypt, or any other country all over the world, are schools that offer a special system of education that is different from any other.
The International Baccalaureate (IB) systems offer a comprehensive learning path that develops students who can think critically, excel in their lives, and are able to face different life challenges. This system offers flexibility in tailoring the students’ learning path.
The International Baccalaureate (IB) system started back in 1968, and it offers 4 main programmes for an age range from 3 to 19. These 4 programs are:
Primary years programme
Middle years programme
Diploma programme
Career-related programme
These programmes are specially designed to offer the students a comprehensive curriculum alongside English, French, Deutsch, Arabic, and other languages.
Lastly, concerning the difference between British schools and IB schools, the following is:
British System offers well-structured, content-focused, and subject-specific curricula.
The IB system offers inquiry-based, well-rounded, and international curricula.
Now that you know the list of IB schools in Egypt, the system, and the curriculum, let us dive deeper into the details of these schools in the following sections.
Read more about International Schools:
International Schools In Egypt: New Cairo, Zayed, Alexandria & More
IB Schools

. The school started in 1982, and it offers many programs such as the IB, British, and American systems.
This school is very well-known for the exceptional educational system it offers from grades 1 to 12.

Uptown International School is one of the international schools in
Uptown Cairo Compound
in Mokattam by Emaar Misr.
It is an IB-based school, which is experienced in offering diverse upscale learning experiences with extensive experience in the educational field.

The school has highly equipped classrooms, a library, a sports area, and offers high-end transportation services for its students.

, Red Sea Governorate.  The school started in 2006, and it offers a wide range of educational programmes.
This school offers the International Baccalaureate system in the Deutsche language, Gemischsprachiges Internationales Baccalaureat (GIB).

.

, Egypt. It is a member of the ACE School Group.
This school offers the IB-accredited Primary Years Programme (PYP), the Middle Years Programme (MYP), and the Diploma Programme (DP).

. The school specializes in offering three IB-accredited programs (primary, middle years, and diploma) in English and French.

.
This school offers a high-end educational experience through various programmes, among which is the IB program.

Best Nurseries in Cairo : A Detailed Guide
==========================================

--- CONTENT ---
Nurseries in Cairo
Nowadays, choosing the best nursery for your kids might be a challenging task. It is not easy when you are trying to find a premium nursery that offers a top-tier educational curriculum. That is why this article is dedicated to giving you a comprehensive overview of the best nurseries in Cairo.
In this article, you will learn what criteria to choose for your kids’ nursery,  and what the most prominent nurseries are all over Cairo.
How To Pick Nurseries in Cairo?
Preschool is reported by the
UNICEF Organization
to have many significant benefits for the child’s physical, mental, and emotional development.  That is why you must find the best nursery possible for your kids.
When you start looking for a suitable nursery for your little ones, there are main criteria that you should consider, which include:
Its proximity to your residence (close and easily accessible or far?)
Transportation options (does it provide a bus to transport your child?)
The age range it serves (does it accommodate kids under 12 months?)
Extracurricular activities (what does it offer aside from the educational curriculum?)
Its facilities (does it have an outdoor playgrounds? music room, and pools? )
In fact, picking a nursery depends upon many factors, not just those mentioned above; however, those are the major criteria for choosing your kids’ nursery or daycare center.
Now that you have an overview of how to choose the nursery, the following sections will give you more information about the top nurseries in Cairo, categorized according to location.
Top Nurseries in New Cairo
Among the most prominent areas in which people look for nurseries in Cairo is New Cairo. These nurseries include the following:
Top Nurseries in New Cairo
Tiny Town Nursery
The nursery is well known for its British Curriculum for EYFS (Early Years Foundation Stage from birth) to under 5 years old. It has a premium location in the El Banafseg District of  New Cairo.
HEI Schools
HEI Schools is one of the most elite nurseries that works in collaboration with the University of Helsinki to offer children from 6 months to 6 years old a high-end Finnish educational curriculum.
Nurseries in Cairo – HEI
Irish School Cairo
Irish School nursery is one of the top nurseries in Cairo, which offers both English and French curricula for children from 6 months to 5 years old.
Explore best Schools in New Cairo

Point 90 Mall
.
Also, don’t forget that you can find Trillium-The Montessori House Nursery and Little Giants Nursery in
Mivida Compound
. Moreover,
Hyde Park Compound
has Maternelle Tournesol Nursery in addition to another branch of Trillium-The Montessori House Nursery.

This nursery offers various educational programs, such as the semi-international section, the Montessori section, and the French section. You can find it in Abou Bakr El Sedik Street in Heliopolis.
Esparanza Educational Pre-school
Esparanza Nursery offers a high-end educational curriculum for children from 3 months to 5 years old. Its curriculum is offered in English, French, German, and Arabic. It is located in Nazih Khalifa Street, next to the British Council.

This well-known nursery has many branches all over Cairo, including Maadi, Rehab, Nasr City, and Fifth Settlement. The nursery offers the British Early Years Foundation Stage (EYFS) curriculum.

This is one of the exceptional nurseries in Heliopolis offering a Montessori educational curriculum. In addition to its educational program, it features a vast selection of development programs for kids. It is found on Khalifa Mamoon Street, in front of the Triumph Hotel.

Best Spots for Snorkeling & Diving in Egypt: for Exquisite Experiences!
=======================================================================

--- CONTENT ---
Snorkeling & Diving in Egypt
Diving through crystal clear water and wandering through its paradise is one of the most bewitching experiences in Egypt. There are boundless destinations in the country for aquatic-loving adventurers! Each spot offers a unique underwater escapade.
This blog will give you all the details about the best diving and snorkeling spots in Egypt. Our list will include various destinations on the Red Sea’s pristine coastline: Dahab, Marsa Allam, and Hurghada.
Grab your diving and snorkeling essentials and be ready for a virtual tour filled with wonders!
Best Diving and Snorkeling Spots in Egypt
Whether you are a local or somebody on the hunt for the best underwater adventures, this article is for you. The captivating shores of Egypt’s Red Sea hold boundless diving and snorkeling spots for both seasoned and casual snorkelers.
Diving and snorkeling are more than adrenaline boosters. These activities offer wanderers and marine lovers matchless opportunities for unforgettable encounters, filled with vibrancy and awe.
The Red Sea coastline is the home of some of the best diving and snorkeling destinations in Egypt, which include:
El Gouna
Hurghada
Soma Bay
Ain Sokhna
Dahab
Marsa Allam
Snorkeling & Diving in Egypt
1. El Gouna, Red Sea
El Gouna is one of Egypt’s world-class destinations, with various wonders to unravel. It is filled with unparalleled spots for various water activities, including snorkeling, diving, and kitesurfing.
El Gouna in Egypt hosts a wide range of destinations for snorkeling and diving, as well as offering the best beach experiences in the country. They include, but are not limited to:
Abu Nahas Reef
, north of
Shedwan El Gouna
. It’s a magical spot where you can enjoy glimpses of vibrant coral gardens and its famous shipwrecks: Giannis D, Carnatic, Chrisoula K, and Kimon M.
Gota Abu Galawa
, suitable for both seasonal and beginner divers and snorkelers. Besides its colorful fish, you can enjoy turtle sightseeing. Red Sea’s pristine waters provide adventure seekers with exceptional visibility to appreciate its spellbinding coral reefs.
Dolphin House
: Diving & snorkeling in Egypt and exploring the Red Sea’s marvels becomes more exquisite when it merges with dolphins!
Careless Reef
, a one-of-a-kind diving site in El Gouna, Egypt, and one of the best spots for
experienced divers
, where you may encounter reef sharks. Moreover, make sure there is
no wind
before you go.
South and North Fanadir Reefs
: Fanadir Marina is one of the best spots in El Gouna, Egypt, for diving, snorkeling, shopping, dining, and making the most out of your escapade! Its vibrant reefs are suitable for beginners as well. It’s characterized by calm waters and infinite marine life.
Diving and Snorkeling in Gouna
Top Diving Centers in Gouna
These were the best diving and snorkeling sites in El Gouna, Egypt. Now, you must be wondering about the top
dive centers
in the area that will help you make the most out of your adventure. They include:
Orca Dive Club
Dream Team Divers
Dive Trek
Additionally, one of the reasons why El Gouna is one of the best diving and snorkeling destinations in Egypt is its prime location, whether you are a local or a traveler. It is:
A 4-hour flight from Europe’s central airports.
5 hours away from Central Cairo.
Best Resorts in El Gouna
Aside from the diving and snorkeling experience in El Gouna, the city is home to many fun activities. In Gouna, you can enjoy a wide range of
activities for a blissful nightlife experience
.
2. Diving in Hurghada, Egypt
Renowned for its buzzing marine life and turquoise, refreshing waters, Hurghada is one of the top diving and snorkeling destinations in Egypt. Some of its best spots are:
Abu Ramada Island
, located south of Giftun National Park. It hosts a wide range of coral creatures, and it is considered one of the richest marine environments in Hurghada.
Get ready to encounter soft and hard coral reefs, angelfish, and turtles!
Small Giftun Drift
, situated 11 km east of Hurghada. Giftun Island offers a blissful, enchanting, and thrilling diving experience in Egypt with its diverse marine life and vibrancy.
Mahmya Island
, located in the southern part of Giftun Island. In addition to being an ideal spot for snorkeling and diving in Egypt, it is known for its natural beauty and magnificent beaches.
Umm Gamar
, south of Hurghada. Its breathtaking coral gardens turn the fantasy of every scuba diver into reality. They would encounter angelfish, butterflyfish, and more, despite being a small island on the Red Sea coastline.
Top Resorts in Hurghada Egypt
It is worth mentioning that you can reach Hurghada by plane (around 1 hour away from Cairo). It is also approximately 32 km away from El Gouna and its popular attractions.
3. Soma Bay
Soma Bay is a world-class destination in Egypt with exquisite diving and snorkeling sites, and they include:
The Seven Pinnacles
, also known as
Tobia Arbaa
. This magnificent spot exhibits seven coral pinnacles, forming a beautiful underwater landscape. It is filled with various reef fish and vibrant coral formations.
Panorama Reef
, where dramatic drop-offs and diversity of marine species blend! It is the home of various sea creatures, including white-tip reef sharks, barracudas, and tuna.
Tobia Hamra
, one of the best diving and snorkeling sites in Hurghada, Egypt. Divers can spot Napoleon tiggerfish and zebra snakes, as well as experience the magic of its magnificent corals.
In addition to the boundless diving spots in Soma Bay, you will be in proximity to the other destinations and the unmatched experiences of Hurghada. It is around 45 km away from Hurghada. Moreover, Somabay is approximately a 1-hour drive to El Gouna.
Top Units in Soma Bay Egypt
Scuba Diving in Tobia Arbaa, Egypt
4. Ain Sokhna
The underwater life of Egypt is filled with wonders, and one of the best snorkeling and diving spots in the country is Ain Sokhna. The city offers a wide range of activities and
things to do
, not just snorkeling spots.
However, it is best known for its premium spots. It offers vacationers a serene escapade on the Red Sea coastline, in addition to the unmatched thrill adrenaline seekers would experience!
Dive into the magical Red Sea and wander through its diverse marine experiences in Ain Sokhna when you visit:
La Siesta Private Beach
, where you can take a glimpse of soft corals. It is a distinctive destination in Egypt, providing a serene environment for snorkeling, diving, and exploring the magic beneath the surface.
Jabal El Sakka Area
, where snorkelers can enjoy the companionship of colorful fish, and sometimes dolphins.
Best Resorts in Ain Sokhna
5. Snorkeling and Diving in Dahab, Egypt
When in Dahab, a magnificent backdrop on Egypt’s Red Sea coastline, there is one thing you need to add to your
must-do
list:
Blue Hole snorkeling
! It is a world-renowned dive site in Egypt, with depths extending to 100 meters.
Blue Hole is a magnificent diving spot in Egypt for
experienced divers
. Furthermore, the magical coral gardens make it an exquisite snorkeling experience. It is filled with diverse marine life, including parrotfish and pufferfish.
In addition to Blue Hole, there are other splendid diving and snorkeling spots in Dahab, Egypt, such as:
The Canyon
, south of Blue Hole, provides vast underwater experiences for free diving in Egypt, as well as snorkeling.
The Lighthouse
is suitable for all levels. Its reefs extend from 0.5 meters to 40 meters. It exhibits a wide variety of marine creatures, such as morays and turtles.
Blue Hole Snorkeling
5. Snorkeling in Marsa Allam
Marsa Allam is a magical spot in Egypt, on the south-eastern shores of the Red Sea, with unlimited snorkeling and diving destinations, including:
Abu Dabbab Bay
, characterized by its shallow waters and is known for its seagrass meadows. It is an astonishing destination in Marsa Allam, Egypt, for snorkeling and diving, where green sea turtles reside.
Moreover, you may encounter guitar sharks
.
Elphinstone Reef
, 30 km from Marsa Allam, where you can run into whitetip sharks along with its beautiful coral gardens.
P.S.:
It is recommended for
experienced divers
.
Daedalus Reef
, a remote site, around 85 km away from Marsa Allam in Egypt, where diving and snorkeling are matchless. Its stunning reef features diverse marine life and steep walls.
Fury Shoals
, with pristine reefs of both hard and soft corals, where you could come across a variety of underwater species.
In addition to the unlimited diving and snorkeling spots on the Red Sea coast in Egypt, the Mediterranean exhibits various destinations for experienced and occasional individuals alike.
Snorkling & Diving in the Northern Coast of Egypt
The northern coast of Egypt is a magnificent backdrop for diving and snorkeling, with many destinations to hit when you’re in the mood for wandering underwater. Some of the top spots are:
Alexandria
, one of the best diving spots in Egypt, is located overlooking the Mediterranean Sea, where you can go on an underwater historical adventure! Its magnificent sites exhibit submerged remains of ancient landmarks, such as Cleopatra’s Palace.
Marsa Matrouh
, situated west of Alexandria in Egypt, an unparalleled choice for diving, snorkeling, and more! It boasts various pristine beaches and beach activities.
North Coast, Egypt, Top Resorts
After telling you where to go diving in Egypt, there are some important factors you need to consider before heading for your adventure.
Diving seasons
Training in case you need it
Regarding the training, there are several dive centers in Egypt where you can learn. It takes at least 4 days to learn.
Dive Centers in Egypt
There are various diving centers in Egypt, found in the areas we mentioned above. They include:
Blue Submarine Diving Center in Marsa Allam
Master Diving Center in Makadi
Scuba Seekers in Dahab
Scuba World Divers in Soma Bay
Scuba World Divers in Mövenpick Resort and Spa, El Gouna
Get ready to experience the rich underwater life of Egypt
.
Nawy is your ultimate spot for all your real estate queries and needs. Check out the best developments in Egypt and their top properties for sale.
Visit Nawy
FAQs about Diving & Snorkeling in Egypt
1. Where to go diving in Egypt?
There are various destinations in Egypt for diving, including but not limited to Marsa Allam, Soma Bay, and El Gouna.
2. When to dive in Egypt?
The best seasons to dive in Egypt include March, May, September, and November.
3. Is there snorkeling in El Gouna?
Yes, El Gouna has various snorkeling destinations, such as Dolphin House.
4. What are the best spots for snorkeling and diving in Egypt?
They include, but are not limited to, Daedalus Reef in Marsa Allam, Tobia Arbaa in Soma Bay, and the south and north Fanadir Reefs in El Gouna.
5. What is the difference between scuba diving and freediving?
Scuba diving is of longer durations, deeper dives, and requires training. On the other hand, freediving grants inexperienced individuals the chance to go underwater for shorter durations.

Best Places to Go on Eid 2026 (+Fun Activities for Kids)
========================================================

--- CONTENT ---
Places to Go in Eid
Eid Al-Adha in Egypt is all about festive energy, joy, and spending time with your loved ones. When it comes to celebrating Eid, you have to navigate through the best places to go with your family and friends to have a blissful time, whether in Cairo or a beachside destination.
This article will tell you about the best places to go in Egypt during Eid, family-friendly activities, and destinations. From parks to aquariums and iconic landmarks, get ready to enjoy a magical holiday.
Eid Activities for Kids
Places to Go in Cairo during Eid with Kids
Places to Go on Eid with Beach Activities
Eid Concerts & Events
Places to Go on Eid
Eid al-Adha is approaching. Whether you are staying in Cairo and looking for the best spots to go with your family, or wondering what activities can make you have a memorable holiday, we have something for you.
There are several activities and family-friendly places to go in Egypt during Eid, including:
Parks
Museums
Resorts and beachside destinations
Leisure destinations, such as aqua parks and aquariums
It is a memorable holiday for kids as well as adults. & picking the right places to go and the best activities you can both engage in would make Eid memorable.
Eid Activities for Kids
Eid Activities for Kids
Make Eid a joyful holiday for your kids and bond with them through amusing activities in the following places (add them to your to-go list all year long, too!):
1.
Wadi Degla Protectorate
in
El Maadi
, where you can go on a family hike and camp in the heart of the city.
Entrance Fee: 3 EGP for Egyptians and 5 EGP for foreigners, and free for kids. When you arrive, there are other fees for activities, including camel rides and private sightseeing tours.
2.
Ski Egypt
in Mall of Egypt, where you can glide with your kids across the snow and greet penguins.
Fee: Ski Egypt has several packages with different fees. They range between 599 EGP and 1500 EGP.
3.
Nile Park
in Maadi, a perfect destination to enjoy a boat ride.
4.
ZED Park
in
ZED El Sheikh Zayed
, hosting various fun activities such as karting and carnival games.
Ticket fees start from 250 EGP.
5.
Magic Galaxy
in City Stars, a huge indoor amusement park with a wide range of fun activities.
6.
Cairo Tower
in Zamalek, where you can enjoy a mouthwatering meal and panoramic views of Cairo.
Entrance Fee: 20 EGP for Egyptians and 70 EGP for foreigners.
7.
Sakkara Pyramids
and the
Pyramids of Giza
, for an enjoyable camel ride.
Entrance Fee: for Sakkara Pyramids: 600 EGP for adults and 300 EGP for students (some specific areas have an extra fee)
For the Pyramids of Giza: 700 EGP for adults and 350 EGP for students.
Furthermore, one of the best places to go during Eid in Egypt is
Hurghada
, and it has boundless family-friendly destinations with joyful activities for kids. You would create unforgettable memories in the
Hurghada Aquarium
and
Makadi Water World
.
Ski Egypt, Mall of Egypt
If you are thinking more of family quality time amid nature, then a park would be a perfect choice. There are various places to go in Cairo during Eid, around Heliopolis,
El Rehab
, Zamalek, and more.
Places to Go in Cairo during Eid with Kids
Parks are magical places, and there are various places to go in Cairo that will make your Eid unforgettable. They include:
Al-Azhar Park
in Salah Salem (On workdays: 40 EGP for adults and 30 EGP for children. From Thursday to Saturday: 50 EGP and 40 EGP)
Al Horreya Garden
in Zamalek.
3.
Family Park
in Al Rehab.
Entrance Fee during Eid: 190 EGP on the first day of Eid, and 210 EGP afterwards.
4.
Japanese Botanical Garden
in Helwan.
Entrance Fee: 5 EGP.
5.
Merryland Park
in Heliopolis.
Entrance Fee: 20 EGP.
If you are looking forward to making Eid family time revolve around history, there are various places to go in Cairo, where you can go down the cultural lane. They include:
Pharaonic Village
in Al Bahr Al Azam, Giza.

in El Fustat, Old Cairo.

(GEM
) in El Remayah Square, Cairo – Alexandria Desert Road.

If you are thinking more of a beachside escapade in Egypt during Eid, we have got you covered with places to go to make your holiday more memorable!
Best Places to Go on Eid with Magnificent Beaches
Some of the best places to go in Egypt during Eid are located on the astonishing Red Sea coastline. Serenity and enjoyment harmoniously blend, with an unmatched touch of luxury.
The following beachside places should be at the top of your list:
Hurghada
There are marvelous places to go in Hurghada for a wonderful Eid, such as Gifton Island Beach and its elite downtown. Additionally, Hurghada is approximately a half an hour drive to El Gouna and its distinctive leisure destinations, restaurants, cafes, and beaches.
El Gouna
El Gouna is one of the first-class destinations in Egypt, overlooking the Red Sea, with various recreational destinations.
For instance, when you are there you can visit Makani Beach Club in El Gouna is a flawless spot to enjoy watersports. El Gouna Golf Club is also a must-visit for golf enthusiasts!
Best Resorts & Destinations in El Gouna
Sharm El Sheikh
One of the elite and splendid places to go in Egypt during Eid is Sharm El Sheikh. It is known for its pristine beaches, luxurious resorts & hotels, and matchless activities and fun destinations.
It is also around 1 hour and 15 minutes away from Cairo by plane.
Sharm El Sheikh
Soma Bay
Enjoy a spectacular Eid holiday in the infinite places to go in Somabay, Egypt. This 2500-acre haven is a world-class, all-year-round destination with unparalleled spots, recreational activities, first-class resorts, hotels, and neighborhoods.
It is 45 km away from Hurghada, and around 5 hours away from Cairo.
Somabay Top Resorts & Destinations
Marsa Alam
Looking forward to diving and snorkeling? You should consider visiting Marsa Alam in Egypt on Eid. It is approximately 8 hours away from Cairo.
Luxor and Aswan
Visiting historical temples and immersing in unmatched serenity would be the highlighted parts of your visit to Luxor and Aswan. You would also enjoy exploring the Nubian culture, wandering through Aswan Souk, and more.

things to do in Aswan
to know more!

Best Gyms in Egypt
Searching for the best fitness center with the best equipment and classes? Whether you are a beginner or a fitness enthusiast, you’ve reached a comprehensive guide about the best gyms in Egypt, from both East & West Cairo to Central Cairo and North Coast.
In this article, we will tell you about some of the best, top-rated gyms in Egypt, the facilities and services they offer, where they are located, and their contact details.
Let’s take a look at Egypt’s fitness scene.
Top Gyms & Fitness Centers in Cairo
North Coast Best Gyms
Best Gyms in Egypt: Cairo Edition
Egypt boasts various unmatched fitness centers and gyms with the best equipment, world-class facilities & services, and a wide range of fitness programs. Whether you are looking for the perfect destination for CrossFit, yoga, or cardio exercise, there are plenty of options.
According to
Mayo Clinic
, exercising benefits go beyond weight management. It helps you manage various health problems, both physically and psychologically, such as high blood pressure and anxiety. Working out not only boosts your energy but also your mood.
That’s why picking the best fitness center for you and your needs is your gateway to a healthy lifestyle, both physically and mentally.
Many well-known fitness & gym brands, such as Gold’s Gym, BeFit 360, and Samia Allouba, have various, perfectly equipped branches across Egypt. Whether you are living in New Cairo, Sheikh Zayed, Madinaty, Zamalek, or Maadi, there is a perfect gym for you.
Many of Cairo’s best compounds feature state-of-the-art fitness facilities.

==========================================================
How to Organize Home Office Like a Pro? (+Checklist Guide)
==========================================================

--- CONTENT ---
Home Office
Trying to figure out how to organize a home office like a pro? You’re not the only one! With many of us working from home these days, having a convenient office that actually feels good to work in has become more important than ever.
Yes, a clean, cozy, and well-organised home office makes a big difference in how focused and calm you feel during the day.
In this guide, we’ll provide you with some simple home office setup tips, a checklist to keep you on track, and a few ideas, like adding plants or cozy lighting, to help your space feel a bit more like you.
When it comes to productivity, even small changes can go a long way.
How to Organize a Home Office?
How to Be Productive in Your Home Office
Best Co-Working Spaces in Egypt
Home Office Checklist​
How to Make an Office in a Small Space
Organize a Home Office 101
According to a Gallup study, 52% of employees in remote-capable roles now work in a hybrid model, while 27% work fully remotely. This means nearly 8 out of 10 people spend at least part of their week working from home.
That is why organizing a home office is no longer optional. It is an essential part of your daily routine, just like your kitchen or living space. A well-planned home office supports better focus, comfort, and work-life balance.
How to Organize a Home Office​? Step by Step
Home Office Setup​
After knowing the importance of organizing a home office, it’s time to ask the main question of the blog:
how to organize a home office?
In this section, we’ll dive into the steps that you should follow to organize a comfortable home office.
1. Choose the Right Spot
The first step you should take is to find a quiet and relaxing spot to create your home office. Setting up your office in a calm and your favourite spot in the house, helping you love work and be more productive.
2. Start with a Clean Slate
Did you choose your favourite spot? Then it’s time to clean this space. Remove items that will interrupt you. Decluttering makes your office clean and tidy.
3. Use a Setup Checklist
Here comes the most important step when it comes to setting up a home office: It’s setting up a home office checklist.
Your checklist should include:
Desk and ergonomic chair
Proper lighting (natural + task lighting)
Cable organisers
Storage bins or drawers
Office essentials (pens, chargers, notebooks)
4. Add Plants to Refresh Your Space
Greenery boosts mood and productivity. Try home office plants like snake plants, pothos, or succulents. The green views and simple decor help you relax and think better.
5. Incorporate Smart Storage Solutions
Home Offices Ideas
Floating shelves, pegboards, and drawer dividers can help keep everything in its place. Use labeled baskets or file organizers to manage paperwork and tools.
6. Style It Your Way
Whether you’re into a cozy DIY setup or something more sleek and modern, make the space feel like you. Hang up some art you love, add a few personal touches, or even go for a bit of luxury with some luxurious finishes.
7. Manage Cords & Tech Gear
Use cable clips, under-desk trays, or zip sleeves to keep wires out of sight. Keep your monitor at eye level, and consider wireless devices to cut clutter.
8. Follow Best Practices for Remote Work
Stick to a schedule and take a short break to keep yourself focused and productive. Avoid the noisy places in your home, and before starting work, write a to-do list.
Home Office Checklist​
You’ve seen why a well-organized home office matters and how to organize a home office​.  Now it’s time to get practical: what should go on your home office checklist? Let’s list it down.
Ergonomic desk and chair
High-speed internet connection
Laptop or desktop computer
External monitor(s)
Keyboard and mouse (Wireless Preferred)
Desk lighting
Cable management tools
Noise-Canceling headphones or microphone
Office storage and organization
Plants or décor for motivation
Setting Up a Home Office Checklist​
How to Make a Home Office in a Small Space?
Are you working with a tight nook or small corner? No problem, you can still build a functional and stylish home office.
Here’s how to organize a home office​ in a small space:
Choose a compact “cloffice” or corner spot
Use a wall-mounted or folding desk
Install vertical shelving or a pegboard
Boost lighting with both natural and task sources
Manage cables and reduce visible tech clutter
Keep décor minimal yet intentional
Select ergonomic, space-saving furniture
Add a plant or two for greenery
Implement weekly or daily declutter routines
Working with a small space doesn’t have to feel limiting. With the right furniture, thoughtful organization strategies, and smart lighting, you can design a compact office that’s functional, calming, and super-efficient.
Speaking of the small spaces, take a look at this blog:
Small Room Design – How To Make Any Room Larger?
How to Be Productive in Your Home Office?
Working from home can be a game-changer, but only if your space and habits support your focus. Between home distractions, blurred work-life boundaries, and a lack of structure, staying productive isn’t always easy.
Here’s how to turn your home office into a productivity spot:
Set a consistent work schedule
Create a distraction-free zone
Use a task management system
Try the Pomodoro technique (Work in focused 25-minute sprints followed by 5-minute breaks. After four sessions, take a longer 15–30 minute break)
Make your space comfortable and inspiring.
Take active breaks
Final Tip: Build a routine that works for you
After knowing some
tips for a productive learning environment in your home
. It’s worth noting that, productivity looks different for everyone. Test different habits, tools, and work styles to find what helps you thrive in your home office.
Coworking Space in Residential Compounds
Mivida New Cairo
While setting up a home office can offer comfort and control, it also comes with its challenges. But what if you could enjoy the flexibility of working near home without the stress of maintaining your own office?
That’s where residential compounds with built-in co-working spaces come in. The top compounds with coworking spaces include, but are not limited to
Mivida Business District
(Within
Mivida New Cairo
) and Espaces in
Beverly Hills
In these compounds, which include fully equipped co-working spaces, everything is for you. So you can focus on your work, collaborate when needed, and draw a clear line between home life and work life.
It’s the perfect solution for remote professionals who crave structure, style, and community without sacrificing convenience.
Speaking of co-working spaces, in the next section, we’ll take a tour of the top co-working spaces in Egypt from New Cairo to Sheikh Zayed and North Coast (
yes, who says that you can’t work by the beach?
).
Best Co-Working Spaces in Egypt
Egypt has a wide array of offices and coworking spaces where you can work or study. From New Cairo to North Coast, these co-working spaces offer you all the amenities to be comfortable and enjoy your work day.

Do You Know How to Organize a Home Office?
At the end of the day, being productive in your home office isn’t about having the most expensive desk or the perfect lighting; it’s about creating a space and routine that support how you work best.
Remember, productivity starts with intention. From setting boundaries to building daily habits, small changes in your home office setup can make a big difference in your performance and well-being.
So take the time to personalize your space, test what works, and build a remote work environment that truly works for you.
Alright, so we’ve got the home office setup part down… But wait, did you pick the home you’re setting it up in?
Kinda important, right?

and your dream office setup
). Easy.

FAQs
What is the best way to organize a home office?
Picking a quiet spot
Decluttering
Using a setup checklist that includes ergonomic furniture, lighting, cable management, and storage.
Adding personal touches like plants and art to boost comfort and focus.
How can I make a home office in a small space?
Use vertical shelving, fold-down desks, and compact furniture. Choose a quiet corner or closet, keep décor minimal, and organize cables.
What should be included in a home office setup checklist?
Ergonomic chair and desk
High-speed internet
Laptop or desktop
External monitor
Wireless keyboard and mouse
Task lighting
Cable organizers
Storage drawers or shelves
Office supplies and plants
How do I stay productive working from home?
Stick to a daily schedule, eliminate distractions, use tools like the Pomodoro technique or time blocking, and take regular breaks. Build a routine that fits your energy levels and create a workspace you enjoy.
What are the best compounds in Egypt with co-working spaces?
Top picks include Mivida New Cairo, VYE SODIC in New Zayed, Sarai New Cairo, and Sila Il Bosco City.

FAQs
When Did Padel Tennis Start?
Padel tennis was started in 1969 by Enrique Corcuera in Acapulco, Mexico. However, the sport started to become popular in Egypt in 2015.
Where are the Best Padel Courts in New Cairo?
New Cairo has many premium padel courts, including Go Padel in Katameya Heights, J Padel in Swan Lake, and Cairo Padel in Mountain View Hyde Park.
Where are the Top Padel Courts in North Coast?
The Rush in Caesar Sodic Ras El Hekma, Mountain View Ras El Hekma, Masyaf Ras El Hekma, or Padel Dose in Amwaj Sidi Abdel Rahman, and Padel Hub in La Vista Bay Ras El Hekma
Is Padel Popular in Egypt?
Yes
How Much is a Padel Court Per Hour?
In Egypt, Padel courts range from 250 EGP up to 700 EGP per hour for one player. The cost depends on the number of players in a team, the location, and the type of court.
