Top 5+ Exclusive Spots for Day Use in Ras Sudr
==============================================

--- CONTENT ---
Day Use in Ras Sudr​
Many people seek solace in taking a day off work and going to enjoy a day use in one of the premium coastal spots. Among these spots is Ras Sudr with its serene setting and pristine beaches. So, if you are on the lookout for an enjoyable, hassle-free day use, you should consider taking a day use in Ras Sudr, Egypt.
The good news? You don’t need to waste your time looking for hotels, searching for prices, and comparing them. Rather, after reading this full guide on the best spots for day use in Ras Sudr, you will be able to seamlessly choose where you want to have your fun and rejuvenating day.
Day Use in Ras Sudr​
Taking a day use in Ras Sudr would be a fun experience if well-planned. Here is a comprehensive list of hotels where you can get a refreshing time:
Golden Beach Resort
La Perla Resort
Matarma Beach Resort
Cecil Beach Resort
Moon Beach Resort
Vintro Inn Beach Resort
Paradise Inn Beach Resort
In the following sections, we’ll get to talk about each of them with a bit of detail.
Day Use in Ras Sudr
Matarma Beach Resort
Matarma Beach Resort is one of the most exceptional spots for a refreshing day use in Ras Sudr. It has a stunning beach zone, and it offers a myriad of beach sports and activities.
Day Use Package: Beach access and the resort’s facilities
Cost: 2000 EGP
Operating Hours: 10:00 am to 7:00 pm
Contact: 19625
Location: 77 km Tunnel Road, El Tour, Ras Matarma, South Sinai
Vintro Inn Beach Resort
Vintro Inn is another premium spot for a day use in Ras Sudr. It is well-known for its exceptional “Nozha Aqua Park” and its mesmerizing beach experience featuring a marina and many beach activities.
This hotel offers two different packages for a day use as follows:
Package 1:
Day Use Package: Beach, pool, and aqua park access
Cost:1000 EGP per person
Operating Hours: 9:00 am to 6:00 pm
Package 2:
Day Use Package: Beach, pool, and aqua park access
Cost: 1500 EGP per person
Operating Hours: 8:00 am to 10:00 pm
Contact: 01010448888
Location: Nozha Beach, Ras Sudr – Ahmed Hamdy Tunnel, Road, South Sinai Governorate
Golden Beach Resort
Golden Beach Resort is one of the exceptional beachfront resorts in Ras Sudr, offering a premium package for day passes. The hotel has it all, various dining options, a kids’ area, and an outdoor pool.
Day Use Package: Beach access
Cost: 800 EGP per person, including a dinner meal
Operating Hours: 10:00 am to 6:00 pm
Contact: 01289686444
Location: Tunnel Road, El Tour 77 km sign, South Sinai Governorate
Day Use Spots in Cairo
Moon Beach Resort
Moon Beach is one of the very well-known resorts for its exceptional beach zone. The resort is also famous for being a beach events venue.
Day Use Package: Beach access
Cost: 500 EGP per person
Operating Hours: 10:00 am to 6:00 pm
Contact: 01201244430
Location: El Tor Road, Makman,98 Km From Ahmed Hamdy Tunnel
La Perla Beach Resort
La Perla Beach Resort is a premium beachfront resort that offers an exceptional day use in Ras Sudr. It features many entertainment and business amenities.
Day Use Package: Beach access with changing rooms
Cost: 150 EGP per person
Operating Hours: 8:00 am to 7:00 pm
Contact: 01286652675
Location: Ras Sudr, 62 km away from Ahmed Hamdy Tunnel
Cecil Beach Resort
Cecil Beach Resort is a stunning, 4-star beach resort. It is one of the first ever resorts that opened its doors in Ras Sudr. The hotel offers many high-end amenities such as a gaming zone, business zone, and an events venue.
Cecil Beach Resort has two day use packages:
Package 1:
Day Use Package: Beach access
Cost: 100 EGP per person
Package 2:
Day Use Package: Pool access
Cost: 150 EGP per person
Operating Hours: 10:00 am to 6:00 pm
Contact: 01011137384
Location: Hotels Street, Next to Fardous Hotel, Ras Sidr, South Sinai Governorate
Paradise Inn Beach Resort
For a mesmerizing day use in Ras Sudr, Paradise Inn Beach Resort should be at the top of your list. It has kids’ and adults’ swimming pools and a relaxed, family-friendly atmosphere.
Day Use Package: pools and beach access
Cost: 350 EGP for adults and 250 EGP for kids for pool access & extra fees for the beach
Operating Hours: 8 am – 10 pm
Contact: 19737
Location: Ras Sedr, South Sinai Governorate 8742101

Are you planning your trip and looking for nightlife venues there? Let’s tell you all about the nightlife in Ras Sudr in the next section.
Nightlife in Ras Sudr
Ras Sudr is not only home to many opulent beach resorts, but it also offers one of the most unique nightlife experiences along the coast.
After the sun goes down, you can enjoy many unforgettable experiences. Ras Sudr’s nightlife destinations and activities include, but are not limited to:
Open-air cinema
Bedouin Night Party
Beach clubs and lounges such as Cuba Cabana in La Hacienda Resort
Camps where you can enjoy stargazing and spend a relaxing night
Ras Sudr Egypt
You might be wondering now about the distance between Cairo and Ras Sudr and the travel time it takes. So, let’s answer these questions in the following section.
Distance from Cairo to Ras Sudr
Ras Sudr is easily accessible and a relatively short drive from Cairo, thanks to its ideal location on the Suez Gulf, Sinai Peninsula, on the Red Sea’s coastline, in South Sinai Governorate.
The distance from Cairo to Ras Sudr is approximately 200 kilometers, which can be covered in a
2.5-hour ride
. The best and shortest route to take is the Martyr Ahmed Hamdi Tunnel  (also known as Ras Sedr – Al Shahid Ahmed Hamdy Tunnel) and its extension
Ahmed Hamdy Tunnel 2
.
Cairo to Rus Sudr-Ahmed Hamdy Tunnel
You already started planning for your trip, but you can’t decide where you should go? The question is usually whether to head for Ras Sudr or Ain Sokhna since both are relatively short drives from Cairo.
So, let us help you to decide on the right destination in the next section.
Ras Sudr VS Ain Sokhna
Many people get stuck in the dilemma of choosing between traveling to Ras Sudr or
Ain Sokhna
. Both cities offer a magnificent coastal spot in which you can enjoy a short trip or a longer stay.
So, where should you go? Ras Sudr or Ain Sokhna?
Ras Sudr
Ain Sokhna
Travel Time from Cairo
2.5 hours
1 hour
Experience
More tranquil – better for relaxation
Vibrant – better for adventures
Best known for
Kitesurfing and water activities
Healing springs and therapeutic venues
Day Use Sports in Ain Sokhna
It is by now clear that both cities are convenient in their own terms. If you are looking for a closer destination, then go for Ain Sokhna. If your priority is relaxation, go for Ras Sudr.
Final Thoughts
In a nutshell, spending a day use in Ras Sudr would be perfect for families and individuals looking for a rejuvenating experience. The city is known for its pristine beaches, events, and nightlife venues.
If you are looking for a beach house in Ras Sudr’s elite resorts, Nawy’s property consultants will assist you.
Explore Top Properties in Ras Sudr
FAQs
How to go from Cairo to Ras Sudr?
You can go by car, taking 2.5 hours, or by bus in 3.5 hours.
Where is Ras Sudr Airport?
It is planned to be located to the north of the Mousa Eyes area (Ayoun Mousa).
Why go to Ras Sudr?
Ras Sudr is conveniently located only 2 hours away from Cairo. It hosts many upscale beach resorts and hotels, is home to many nightlife venues, and has a lovely events venue by the beach.
What is better Ras Sudr or Ain Sokhna?
Depending on your priority, if you want a short drive and a therapeutic experience, it is Ain Sokhna. If you want a more meditative environment, it is Ras Sudr.

Top 10+ Places To Visit in Egypt for a Mesmerizing Vacation

--- CONTENT ---
Best Places to Visit in Egypt
Egypt, where history meets nature, creates a magical all-year-round destination for anyone planning a trip to the country of beauty. Whether you are a local looking forward to a unique journey around the country or a foreign visitor planning your vacation, this comprehensive guide to the best 10+ places to visit in Egypt is for you.
In this article, you will get to know all about the top cities and destinations to visit in Egypt, their exact locations, and all their details. We promise that by the end, you will have planned your next visit to Egypt in detail.
Winter Destinations
Summer Destinations
Family Destinations
Honeymoon Destinations
Top Places To Visit in Egypt
Egypt is teeming with numerous places that seamlessly blend the beauty of nature with rich heritage and history. The country has it all, from tourist places to local outings, all within one border.
That is why planning a trip to or around Egypt is one of the most fun tasks. This is why, in this article, we’ll be giving you a combination of main cities in which you can enjoy your stay, in addition to destinations that you can visit.
In this list, you will find sightseeing places, shopping venues, and more. The list of the best places to visit in Egypt includes the following:
Luxor & Aswan
Ain Sokhna
Siwa

Also, after taking a glimpse at each of these places, we will tell you the best places for families in Egypt and the top honeymoon destinations in Egypt.
Winter places in Egypt
1. Luxor & Aswan
To begin with, Luxor and Aswan are two of the top and most attractive places to visit in Egypt. Both cities are located in the southern part of Egypt, by the Nile, and they are home to many monuments and archaeological sites, which is why,
though they are two separate destinations, they are always treated as one.
In Luxor and Aswan, you can visit the following places:
The High Dam of Aswan
Unfinished Obelisk in Aswan
Karnak Temple in Luxor
Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut in Luxor
Abu Simbel Temple in Aswan
Valley of the Kings Tombs area in Luxor
Rams Road Luxor
Luxor Museum
Moreover, visitors can take a cruise or even a full stay on the River Nile, and enjoy a hot air balloon ride. (
Check out more things to do in Aswan
).
Luxor
2. Ain Sokhna
Ain Sokhna, Egypt, is a mesmerizing coastal heaven that should be on your must-visit places in Egypt. Thanks to its weather, its proximity, and its beautiful beaches, Ain Sokhna is an all-year-round destination.
In Ain El Sokhna, visitors can:
Enjoy an elite stay in one of the opulent beach resorts or hotels, such as
IL Monte Galala
Go for a mountain-top adventure on Al Galala Mountain
Take advantage of the therapeutic effect of the Sulphur Springs
Go for adventures and a safari in the desert
Enjoy beach activities such as kitesurfing
Also, Ain Sokhna is known for Al Galala City, which is popular for its mountain-top views and elite resorts.
Ain Sokhna
Explore Top Ain Sokhna Properties
3. Siwa
Siwa is among the best, well-known places to visit in Egypt, especially in winter. Also known as Siwa Oasis, it is an ancient, isolated oasis in the Western Desert of Egypt, in which the Berber (Amazigh) culture prevails.
One can find
many activities to do in Siwa Oasis
to enjoy, such as:
Visiting the Salt Lakes and Cleopatra Eyes
Sightseeing Shali Fortress
Camping
Going Safari
Going
shopping and
antique and souvenir buying from the main central”Souk”
Catching the sunset from one of the islands
Siwa
4. Dahab and Nuweiba
Dahab and Nuweiba are two different cities, yet they give off the same nomadic, tranquil vibes. Both are well-known destinations for those who want to enjoy a calm all-year-round vacation by the beach.
In Dahab and Nuweiba, one can:
Hiking on one of the popular mountains, such as St. Catherine’s Mountain
Visit the well-known Blue Hole snorkeling spot in Dahab
Enjoy camping by the beach
Go stargazing
Go safari
Do kitesurfing
Enjoy diving
Dahab

5. Hurghada
The City of Hurghada is one of the mesmerizing coastal spots by the Red Sea, and it is one of the stunning places to visit in Egypt. The city stretches along 40 km of coastline, within which many local and tourist activities are offered.
Visitors can enjoy many
things to do in Hurghada
, which include but are not limited to the following:
High-end stay in one of the top-rated hotels and beach resorts, such as Azur Hotels
Water activities such as snorkeling, boat tours, dolphin watching, and aqua park activities

Mountain biking
Desert safari and stargazing
Do kite surfing
Visit Hurghada National Museum
Hurghada
6. North Coast

7. El Gouna
El Gouna, Egypt, is another one of the most stunning places to visit in Egypt. Also, El Gouna is a mesmerizing coastal spot by the Red Sea.
The city is home to many upscale beach resorts where visitors can enjoy an elite stay. Also, in El Gouna, visitors can enjoy the following activities:
Enjoying an elevated stay in one of the five-star hotels, such as the Ancient Sands Golf Resort and Hotel

Explore Top El Gouna Properties
8
. Alexandria
Alexandria City, where the beauty of the beach meets the magic of Roman and Greek history. The city peacefully rests in the western Delta of the Mediterranean coast in northern Egypt.
Top activities in Alexandria
that one can do include:
Sightseeing unique sights such as the Lighthouse, Catacombs of Kom El Shoqafa (Mound of Shards), and Qaitbay Citadel
Enjoy a tour of the Alexandria Bibliotheca
Visit museums such as the Greco-Roman Museum and the Royal Jewelry Museum
Enjoy a day use in one of the top-rated hotels (check out the
top day use hotels in Alexandria
)
Shop in any of the high-end malls, such as City Centre Mall
Enjoy an elevated stay at one of the resorts or five-star hotels, such as Paradise Inn
Places to Visit in Egypt
9. Saqqara Complex
The Saqqara Complex is one of the best and most attractive historical places to visit in Egypt. The area is 40 km southeast of Cairo, and it is home to many historical monuments, such as:
Well-known 3 Great Giza Pyramids
Sphinx
Step Pyramid of Djoser
Serapeum, a series of underground galleries
In Saqqara, visitors can:
Ride a horse or a camel
Visit the monuments, museums, and sightseeing sites
Buy antiques and souvenirs for their loved ones
10. Coptic Cairo
Coptic Cairo is known to be one of the major attractions in the Old Cairo area. The Coptic Cairo is home to many historical monuments and sightseeing venues, which include, but are not limited to:
Coptic Museum
Complex of 7 ancient churches
Babylon Fortress
11. El Mu’izz’s (Islamic) Cairo
El Mu’izz’s Cairo, or Islamic Cairo, is another mesmerizing, historical part of Old Cairo. It is also one of the stunning places to visit in Egypt. The area hosts a wide collection of historical mosques, such as Amr Ebn Al Ases’ and Sultan Hassan Mosque.
The area is also home to many fun activities, such as:
Souvenir and antique buying
Sightseeing
Try out street food
Walking through Khan Al Khalili Street
Enjoying a day out in Al Azhar Park
12. Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM)
The Grand Egyptian Museum is one of the top-rated places to visit in Egypt. This exceptional museum was opened in a historical ceremony on November 1st 2025. The Grand Egyptian Museum hosts many unique historical and archaeological galleries and pieces.
Visitors of the museum will be able to:
Watch unprecedented historical pieces
Know more about ancient Egyptian history
Shop antiques and souvenirs
13. The National Museum of Egyptian Civilization (NMEC)
The National Museum of Egyptian Civilization is one of the recent outstanding places to visit in Egypt. Its opening in April 2021 through The Pharaohs’ Golden Parade was a one-of-a-kind event that was streamed worldwide.
Visiting the National Museum of Egyptian Civilization allows visitors to take a tour through:
Main Gallery, in which unique pieces from different historical ages are exhibited
Textile Gallery, in which the visitor is taken through the journey of the textile industry in Ancient Egypt
Royal Mummies Gallery, where famous pharaohs’ mummies are exhibited
Dye House, which is a unique archaeological site that dates back to the Fatimid period
14. Wadi Degla Protectorate
If you love adventures and nature-based spots, then you will fall in love with Wadi Degla Protectorate. This unique destination that you really need to visit during your stay in Egypt is only 10 km away from Cairo, at the connection point of Maadi and Ain Sokhna.
In Wadi Degla Protectorate, one can:
Go camping and hiking
Enjoy wilderness walks & biking trails
Go for group picnics amidst the desert
15. Ras Mohamed Nature Reserve
Ras Mohamed Nature Reserve is one of the most magnificent protected areas and parks in Sharm El Sheikh. It is at the southernmost tip of the Sinai Peninsula, overlooking the intersection point of the Gulf of Suez and the Gulf of Aqaba.
The area hosts:
Stunning coral reefs
Wildlife
Many beautiful beaches
16. Sharm El Sheikh
Sharm El Sheikh is one of the mesmerizing coastal spots in South Sinai. It is known for its natural reserves as well as its luxurious hotels and lavish stays.
The city is home to various landmark spots to visit, such as Sharks Bay, Neama Bay, Souk, and protectorates.
Explore Top Things To Do in Sharm El Sheikh (Locations Included)
By now, you know all about the top 16 places to visit in Egypt, but what if you want to tailor your trip so your kids have a fun place to enjoy? Let us tell you where you can go with them.
Best Places to Visit in Egypt with Family
Picking the suitable place for a family outing or even a family vacation is not an easy task. You prioritize your children’s safety, so you think of places that are not very crowded, as well as hosting food and beverage options.

FAQs
What To Visit in Egypt?
In Egypt, you can visit cities such as Alexandria, Hurghada, Siwa, and Dahab. You can also visit places such as the Saqqara Complex, Grand Egyptian Museum, and Wadi Degla Protectorate.
Which City to Visit in Egypt?
Egypt has many stunning cities to visit, such as Luxor – Aswan, North Coast, Sahl Hasheesh, Marsa Alam, and El Gouna.
How Much is it to Visit The Pyramids in Egypt?
It costs EGP 700 (approximately USD 14.50 – according to today’s conversion rate) for foreigners and 60 EGP for Egyptians.
How Long to Spend in Egypt for a Visit?
The time spent in Egypt on a visit depends on your plan. If you are planning on visiting cities, then at least you should stay for 4 days to a week. If you plan on just visiting places, then you could stay a day or two.
Where is the Most Visited Place in Egypt?
The tourist places, such as the Great Pyramids, are the most visited in Egypt.
What to do in Egypt in 5 Days?
Staying in Egypt for 5 days allows you to go for a tour around the major sightseeing venues, such as Karnak Temple in Luxor or Giza Pyramids, or go for a luxurious stay in one of the coastal cities, such as Gouna.

Best Museums in Cairo & Others in Egypt (Maps & Fees Included)

--- CONTENT ---
Best Museums in Cairo
It’s undeniable that you have heard about the long-awaited opening of the Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM) on November 1st. Perhaps that made you excited to visit this museum and the other best museums in Cairo and Egypt. This is why, in this article, we will help you plan your next visit.
Throughout this comprehensive guide, you will get to know all about the best museums in Cairo and other cities in Egypt as well.
Also, you will be able to know their details, fees, and locations, so you can easily plan your visit.
So, set back, and get ready to plan a mesmerizing museum visit all around Egypt!
Museums in Cairo
Other Museums in Egypt
Museums in Egypt: A Long Story
This article is going mainly to tackle the best museums in Cairo and other museums all around Egypt. But why Cairo specifically?
Cairo is one of the oldest cities in Egypt. It has stories and history-long monuments, not just museums. Also, the city has witnessed different historical eras and dynasties.
Also, Cairo hosts most of Egypt’s museums. It is especially a significant host with the latest addition to its list with the soon opening of Grand Egyptian Museum. This is why, in this article, we’ll be focusing mainly on following Cairo’s museums, then taking a glimpse at other museums in Egypt.
Best Museums in Cairo
The list of the best museums in Cairo includes the following:
Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM)
National Museum of Egyptian Civilization (NMEC)
Egyptian Museum in Cairo (EMC)
Abdeen Palace Museum
Manial Palace Museum
Museum of Islamic Art in Cairo
Coptic Museum
Gayer Anderson Museum
Egyptian Air Force Museum
Agricultural Museum
Mahmoud Mokhtar Museum
Also, it should be noted that these museums welcome visitors of all ages, and children under 6 years are free of charge.
Now, let us talk about each of them in a bit of detail.
Cairo Museums
1. Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM)
First and foremost, the one that has been taking the hype lately, the Grand Egyptian Museum, which is expected to be one of the best museums in Cairo. It should be noted that the museum started operating and closed its doors on October 15th in preparation for the grand soft opening that took place on
November 1st
.
The opening ceremony was a mystical event that was streamed and watched all over the world. It was hosted by the well-known Egyptian actress, Sherihan, alongside a group of young, well-known faces. Also, the Cairo Celebration Choir performed an aorchestral piece under the supervision fo Nayer Naguie.
Also, the Grand Egyptian Museum is expected to be one of the major landmarks in the west of Cairo, on the Cairo-Alexandria Desert Road, close to many upscale compounds,
such as:
Pyramid Hills in 6th of October City
Cairo Gate in El Sheikh Zayed
It hosts the Grand Staircase Gallery with over 60 remarkable artifacts, twelve exhibition halls, the Hanging Obelisk, and a Grand Hall.
It should be noted that many top-rated real estate developers contribute to the opening ceremony of the museum.
For example:
Hassan Allam Properties
Talaat Moustafa Group
Orascom Construction
Operating hours
: 8:30 am to 7:00 pm (hours might differ between different galleries and from weekdays and weekends, but generally operating hours start at 8:30 am)
Fees for adults
: 200 EGP for Egyptians and 1220 EGP (25.63 $) for foreigners
Location
: Cairo – Alexandria Desert Road
Check out the top 10+ things to do in West Cairo
GEM Nights
Following its opening, the well-known United Media Services (UMS) company announced the “GEM Nights” global music season. This concert series is announced as part of the “Egypt Nights” festivities, supported by the Ministries of Tourism & Antiquities, Culture, and the Egyptian Tourism Promotion Authority. The GEM concerts include:
Croatian cellist Hauser’s concert held on December 4th, 2025
Egyptian soprano Fatma Said’s concert held on December 5th, 2025
American R&B singer Brian McKnight’s concert held on December 12th, 2025
British singer-songwriter Calum Scott’s concert held on January 9th, 2026
The long-awaited concert of Bryan Adams held on February 13th,2026
2. National Museum of Egyptian Civilization (NMEC)
The National Museum of Egyptian Civilization (NMEC) has been one of the best museums in Cairo since its opening in November 2021. With a magical parade of pharaonic mummies, the National Museum of Egyptian Civilization started operating, offering one main gallery, the royal mummies hall, and the textile gallery.
Operating hours
: 9:00 am to 5:00 pm
Fees for adults
: 90 EGP for Egyptians and 550 EGP (11.55 $) for foreigners
Location
: Ein as Seirah,
Old Cairo
National Museum of Egyptian Civilization NMEC
3. Egyptian Museum in Cairo (EMC)
The
Egyptian Museum
in Tahrir, Cairo, is considered the first ever national museum in the Middle East. Its concept started linguirng since the 1850s until it was officially acclaimed and opened in 1902.
This unique museum contains more than 100,000 artifacts. Currently, this museum is undergoing a renovation project to bring it back to its good old days.
Operating hours
: 9:00 am to 4:00 pm
Fees for adults
: 30 EGP for Egyptians and 550 EGP (11.55 $) for foreigns
Location
: El-Tahrir Square, Ismailia, Qasr El Nil
4. Abdeen Palace Museum
The Abdeen Palace Museum stands out as one of the best museums to visit in Cairo. It has been the Egyptian royal household’s primary official residence from 1874 until the July Revolution in 1952.
The palace was originally built at the orders of Khedive Ismail. Later, it was transformed into a museum to show its magical pieces. Currently, it hosts many sub-museums and showrooms.
Operating hours
: 9:00 am to 3:00 pm
Fees for adults
:20 EGP for Egyptians and 100 EGP ( 2.10 $) for foreigners
Location
: El-Gomhoreya Square, Rahbet Abdin, Abdeen
Abdeen Palace Museum
5. Manial Palace Museum
Manial Palace Museum, also known as the Mohamed Ali’s Palace Museum, is one of the majestic museums to visit in Egypt, also related to the Mohamed Ali Family. This unique palace is known for exhibiting various styles of Islamic architecture.
It blends the modern Islamic style with Persian and Mamluk elements, in addition to Syrian, Moroccan, and Andalusian motifs, and Ottoman style.
Operating hours
:9:00 am to 5:00 pm
Fees for adults
: 20 EGP for Egyptians and 200 EGP (4.2 $) for foreigns
Location
: 1 Al Saraya, Al Manyal Ash Sharqi, Old Cairo
6. Islamic Art Museum
Another well-known museum in Egypt is the Islamic Art Museum, which was officially opened in 1903 to showcase exceptional pieces of Islamic art. The museum currently hosts more than 100000 artifacts.
Also, the museum was closed in 2014 due to an explosion targeting the opposite building (Cairo Security Directorate). It was reopened in 2017 after renovation.
Operating hours
: 9:00 am to 5:00 pm
Fees for adults
: 20 EGP for Egyptians and 270 EGP (5.68 $) for foreigners
Location
: Port Said, Ghayt Al Adah, Al-Darb Al-Ahmar
Islamic Art Museum
7. Coptic Museum
The Coptic Museum is one of the best and most unique museums in Cairo. It was established by Marcus Simaika in 1908 to be a home for major Coptic Christian artifacts.
Currently, it hosts a wide collection of unique pieces that date back to the 1st Century A.D..It is also home to more than 25 galleries.
Operating hours
: 9:00 am to 5:00 pm
Fees for adults
: 20 EGP for Egyptians and 230 EGP (4.83 $) for foreigners
Location
: 3 Mari Gerges, Kom Ghorab, Old Cairo
8. Gayer Anderson Museum
The Gayer Anderson Museum is one of the best and most popular museums in Cairo. The museum is made up of two Ottoman houses in which the English Doctor Gayer Anderson lived and requested from the Assembly of Preserving Arab Antiquities to collect special antiques from different eras.
Operating hours
: 9:00 am to 5:00 pm
Fees for adults
: 20 EGP for Egyptians and 100 EGP (2.1 $) for foreigners
Location
: Ahmad ibn Tulun, Sayyida Zeinab
Gayer Anderson Museum
9. Air Force Museum
The Egyptian Air Force Museum, also known as Almaza Airport Museum, is one of the most unique museums in Egypt. It exhibits a wide selection of wartime antiques.
The museum hosts a vast collection of aircraft that were used in different wars throughout Egyptian history. Also, it has a mesmerizing outdoor area where ancient Egyptian antiques are showcased.
Operating hours
: 9:00 am to 3:00 pm except Tuesdays
Fees for adults
: 30 EGP for Egyptians and 100 EGP (2.1 $) for foreigners
Location
: Almaza Airport, El Nozha, Heliopolis
10. Agricultural Museum
The Agricultural Museum is one of the most well-known museums in Cairo. It opened its doors in 1938, and in 2024, it was temporarily closed to undergo renovations.
The museum hosts a wide selection of scientific collections, different plants, and narrates the history of ancient Egyptian agriculture. When the renovation is done, it is planned that the museum will open its doors, adding new sub-museums to its galleries.
Operating hours
: 9:00 am to 3:00 pm except Fridays
Fees for adults
: 10 EGP for Egyptians and 50 EGP (1 $) for foreigners
Location
: Dokki
Agricultural Museum
11. Mahmoud Mukhtar Museum
The Mahmoud Mukhtar Museum is one of the best museums to visit in Cairo. It opened its doors back in 1934, to be home for the Egyptian sculptor and artist Mahmoud Mukhtar’s works and sculptures.
The Egyptian Authorities opened this museum to signify their appreciation for the artist’s works. Moreover, it now hosts Mukhtar’s personal belongings side by side with his main sculptures.
Operating hours
: 9:00 am to 4:00 pm except Fridays
Fees for adults
: 5 EGP for Egyptians and 50 EGP (1$) for foreigners
Location
: El Tahrir, Zamalek
By now, you know almost all of Cairo’s major and best museums; it is high time we talked a little bit about other museums found all over Egypt. In fact, Egypt is filled to the brim with many museums in other governorates than Cairo, including Giza, Marsa Matrouh, and Suez.
Helwan Corner Museum
Aside from Cairo, there are many unique museums found in different governorates all around Egypt, one of which is the Helwan Corner Museum. This one is also known as Farouk’s Corner.
This unique museum, that is created on the Nile’s bank, was built in 1942 as a rest house for Farouk’s royal family members.
It reopened
in 2016 after its renovation to be a museum that exhibits statues, souvenirs, photographs, oil paintings of members of the royal family, and a collection of replicas of the furniture discovered in King Tutankhamun’s tomb.
Operating hours
: 9:00 am to 3:00 pm
Fees for adults
: 20 EGP for Egyptians and 100 EGP (2.1$) for foreigners
Location
: Nile Corniche, Helwan El Balad
Helwan Corner Museum
Royal Jewelry Museum
Another major, well-known museum in Egypt is the Royal Jewelry Museum in
Alexandria
. This unique-style museum was first built in 1919 as a residence for Princess Fatma Al Zahraa, descendant of the Mohamed Ali Royal family.
Later, it was transformed into a museum that holds royal jewelry pieces of most of the family’s ladies. It hosts more than 11,500 artifacts and jewelry pieces.
Operating hours
: 9:00 am to 5:00 pm
Fees for adults
: 20 EGP for Egyptians and 110 EGP (2.31 $) for foreigners
Location
: San Stefano, El Raml, Zizinya
El Alamein Military Museum
El Alamein Military Museum in
Al Alamein City
is one of the most spectacular museums in North Coast. It opened its doors in 1965 upon a presidential decree by the late President Gamal Abd El-Nasser. The museum was created to commemorate World War II remnants in which Egypt took part.
Also, this museum exhibits many indoor and outdoor galleries, as well as many pieces that date back to the real soldiers in the war. The museum mainly focuses on
the remains of the Al Alamein Battle.
Operating hours
: 9:00 am to 4:00 pm
Fees for adults
: 20 EGP for Egyptians and 100 EGP (2$) for foreigners
Location
: 105-kilometer sign en route Alexandria-Matrouh road, south of the International Coastal Road
Al Alamein Military Museum
What was mentioned above does not construct the only group of museums in
Cairo or Egypt in general.
Rather, it was just a tini-tiny part of the numerous museums in Egypt that mount over 80, according to the latest count.
Don’t forget that there are many
mesmerizing places to visit in Egypt
, where you can enjoy your time. Aside from museums, the country offers many vibrant spots, such as:
Khan El Khalili
Wadi Degla Protectorate
Saqqara Complex

Red Sea vs Mediterranean Sea: Invest or Vacation in Egypt?
==========================================================

--- CONTENT ---
Red Sea vs Mediterranean Sea
Two Seas, One Country, Endless Charm!
This is the concept of our blog today about: Red Sea vs Mediterranean Sea.
Few places on Earth can claim the natural diversity and beauty that Egypt does. Blessed by both the Red Sea Riviera and the Mediterranean Sea, Egypt’s coastlines offer travelers, residents, and investors a unique blend of crystal-clear seawater, luxury resorts, and thriving real estate opportunities.
From the coral-rich depths of the Gulf of Aqaba to the chic marinas of El Alamein, each side of Egypt’s coastline tells a different story, one of adventure, relaxation, and investment potential.
But which coastline offers the best experience? Let’s dive in.
Red Sea vs Mediterranean Sea: What Makes Them Different?
When comparing Red Sea vs Mediterranean Sea, the contrast isn’t just about geography — it’s about lifestyle, climate, and the kind of experience you’re looking for.
The Red Sea, lying between Africa and the Arabian Peninsula, feels like a year-round tropical escape.
Its calm, warm sea water and colorful coral reefs make it one of the most popular destinations for divers and marine lovers worldwide.
Cities like Sharm El Sheikh, Hurghada, and
El Gouna
thrive on adventure, eco-luxury, and the allure of the Red Sea Riviera lifestyle.
In contrast, the Mediterranean Sea, stretching along Egypt’s northern edge near the Nile Delta, offers an entirely different rhythm. It’s where cosmopolitan beach life meets modern architecture and glamorous summer vibes.
North Coast – or “Sahel,” as locals call it – is Egypt’s playground for high-end leisure, chic marinas, and world-famous resorts like Marassi and Zahra.
In short:
The Red Sea is your serene, all-year paradise for diving, exploration, and peaceful living.
The Mediterranean is your summer luxury escape — elegant, social, and perfect for vacation homeowners.
Let’s explore both worlds in depth, from their cities and activities to their real estate investment potential.
The Charm of Egypt’s Red Sea
Orascom Development El Gouna
The Red Sea stretches along Egypt’s eastern border, separating Africa from the Arabian Peninsula. Known for its calm waters, vibrant marine life, and world-class diving sites, the Red Sea is a paradise for those seeking adventure and tranquility alike.
Its coastline includes iconic destinations. The Red Sea cities in Egypt​ include:
Sharm El Sheikh
Hurghada
Marsa Alam
El Gouna
Dahab
Each city blends modern comfort with natural wonder. But what makes the Red Sea in Egypt so special and a significant destination? That’s what we’ll be talking about below.
If you’re passionate about exploring Egypt’s underwater treasures, you’ll find plenty of world-class diving sites across the Red Sea Riviera, from the coral reefs of Ras Mohammed National Park to the vibrant marine life in the Gulf of Suez and Marsa Alam.
Discover the best spots for snorkeling and diving in Egypt and experience the beauty of the Red Sea like never before:
Best Spots for Snorkeling & Diving in Egypt: for Exquisite Experiences!
Popular Destinations in the Red Sea

Ras Mohammed National Park, which is located on the southern tip of the
Sinai Peninsula.
The
Gulf of Suez
and
Gulf of Aqaba
boast pristine waters and spectacular underwater views, ideal for snorkeling, windsurfing, and kitesurfing.
Moreover, the Red Sea isn’t just a tourist haven; it’s also becoming one of Egypt’s top real estate investment destinations. Cities like El Gouna combine sustainability, luxury, and vibrant community life.

Allure of the Mediterranean Coast (El Sahel, Egypt)
On the opposite side of the Nile Delta lies Egypt’s Mediterranean Sea coastline — stretching from Alexandria to Marsa Matrouh. This region, often referred to as
North Coast
, has evolved into Egypt’s most glamorous summer destination.
El Sahel, Egypt, has a wide range of advantages that make it one of the best summer destinations in the world. Some of the best advantages are:
1. Nature & Lifestyle
The Mediterranean Sea offers a completely different vibe. Its seawater is cooler, with turquoise shades that shimmer against white sands. The Mediterranean climate, mild winters, and breezy summers make it a seasonal haven for Cairo’s elite.
Beyond beach life, the region is close to cultural gems like Siwa Oasis, Alexandria’s historic landmarks, and the Nile Delta.
2. Real Estate & Resorts
Over the past decade, North Coast has transformed into an investment powerhouse. From family chalets to seafront villas, developers have redefined Egyptian luxury living.

===============================================================
Things to Do in Makadi Bay: Full Guide to Diving, Beaches & Fun
===============================================================

--- CONTENT ---
Makadi Bay
Planning your sunny escape? You’ve picked a winner! Makadi Bay, nestled near Hurghada, is truly a jewel on the Red Sea coast, blending stunning natural beauty with endless opportunities for fun and relaxation. If you are looking for the absolute things to do in Makadi Bay, you’ve come to the right place!
Get ready to dive into the famous crystal-clear waters right off the main beach; it’s the perfect start to any vacation. But Makadi Bay is about more than just sunbathing. It’s a paradise for marine enthusiasts, with the vibrant Makadi Bay coral reef practically on your doorstep. Just grab your mask and fins!
Families will find plenty of activities to keep the kids busy, from the thrilling slides at Aquapark Makadi Bay to safe, sandy beach playgrounds.
Looking for adventure in Makadi Hurghada?
Then it’s time to read this full guide about the top things to do in Makadi Bay, from adventure and safari to marina life and snorkelling.
Things to Do in Makadi Bay
So, you’ve heard the whispers about Makadi Bay things to do? Have you seen the stunning photos of turquoise waters and golden sands? Trust me, the reality is even better.
Makadi Bay, nestled on Egypt’s Red Sea coast, is far more than just a cluster of beautiful resorts. It’s a genuine slice of paradise, a dedicated haven for water sports lovers, sun worshippers, and families looking for that perfect, all-inclusive escape.
The main topic here is Makadi Bay things to do, and we are going to dive deep (literally and figuratively) into every incredible experience this beautiful area offers.
Forget the hustle and bustle; this destination, close to
Hurghada
, offers a tranquil yet action-packed holiday with its crystal clear waters, stunning Makadi Bay coral reef, and that unforgettable beauty of the Red Sea.
The Underwater World: Diving & Snorkeling in Makadi Bay
Marine Life
Let’s be honest: for many, the Red Sea means one thing: an underwater kingdom that’s simply out of this world. Makadi Bay is a globally-renowned hub for marine enthusiasts, boasting a breathtaking network of vibrant coral and diverse fish.
If you’re looking for the absolute top Makadi Bay things to do, grabbing your snorkel or scuba gear is undoubtedly number one.
The Phenomenal Makadi Bay Coral Reef
What sets Makadi Bay apart is its
house reef
—an easily accessible and spectacularly healthy coral barrier right off the shore of many of the main resorts. This makes spontaneous snorkeling a breeze!
Makadi Bay’s House Reef:
You don’t need a boat trip to see incredible sights. Many resorts, like Grand Makadi and Fort Arabesque, have jetties that extend over the reef, offering direct access to the drop-off.
You’ll instantly be surrounded by a kaleidoscope of colorful reef fish, graceful rays, and maybe even a gentle sea turtle. It’s perfect for all ages and skill levels.
North Reef vs. South Reef:
The North Reef (often accessible near the Grand Makadi hotel) is widely celebrated for its sheer vibrancy, while the South Reef (near Stella Makadi Resort) offers a slightly different, equally beautiful environment with vast expanses of coral.
To explore more of the incredible Red Sea and see how Makadi Bay stacks up, check out the blog:
Best Spots for Snorkeling & Diving in Egypt: for Exquisite Experiences!
Top Diving & Snorkeling Spots (Beyond the Bay)
While the house reef is amazing, venturing out on a day trip will unlock even more spectacular sites, perfect for both scuba diving and deep-sea snorkeling.
Giftun Island National Park
Giftun Island National Park
This is a must-do boat excursion. Cruising out to the Giftun Islands offers pristine, uncrowded snorkeling spots with exceptionally clear visibility. It’s a full day of sun, sea, and exploring the amazing reefs, an absolute highlight among the Makadi Bay things to do.
Shaab Abu Hashish
This is a famous, shallow reef location that’s perfect for both beginners and experienced divers. Its ‘grass’ area is known to attract dugongs, a once-in-a-lifetime sight!
Dolphin House (Sha’ab Samadai)
Dolphin House (Sha’ab Samadai)
Imagine snorkeling alongside a pod of wild dolphins!
This crescent-shaped reef is a natural home for large numbers of dolphins, offering an ethical and magical encounter with the marine life Makadi Bay is known for.
The Shoreline Charm: Makadi Bay Beach & Activities
After a morning exploring the underwater world, it’s time to enjoy the Makadi Bay beach itself. The bay is characterized by its wide, soft, golden sands and calm waters, making it ideal for relaxation and play.
The area’s protected cove design ensures the waters remain quiet and perfect for swimming, especially for children.
Sun, Sand, and Relaxation: Makadi Bay Beach
The beaches are universally clean and well-maintained, usually offering all the amenities you’d expect, from sun loungers and parasols to beach bars.
Jaz Makadi Saraya Beach:
Known for its long stretch of white sand and excellent facilities, this beach is perfect for families, offering both relaxation and easy access to water sports.
Fort Arabesque Beach:
This resort boasts one of the longest and most tranquil beaches in the bay, a beautiful spot to enjoy the desert atmosphere as it meets the Red Sea. This is your spot for ultimate quiet time.
Makadi Spa Beach:
Designed for a more tranquil, adult-focused experience, this is the perfect beach for a romantic sunset stroll or simply soaking up the sun’s rays in peace.
Water Sports and Beach Thrills
If simply lying down isn’t quite your speed, the Makadi Bay beach is a launchpad for endless thrills!
Windsurfing and Kitesurfing:
The Red Sea is famous for its steady breezes, making Makadi Bay an excellent spot for riding the wind. Lessons and rentals are available from various centres.
Kayaking and Stand-Up Paddleboarding (SUP):
A more relaxed way to enjoy the crystal clear waters and get a gentle workout, paddleboarding and kayaking are popular activities, often available directly through your resort.
Boat Trips and Excursions:
Beyond the diving trips, you can book a romantic sunset sailing trip or a high-speed adventure. Consider a trip on the
Royal Seascope Submarine Cruise
for a unique, dry-view experience of the reef, perfect for those who prefer to stay dry!
Family Fun & Exhilarating Day Trips at Makadi Hurghada
Makadi Water World
Makadi Bay is a fantastic destination for families, offering a great balance of relaxation and activities to keep kids—and adults—of all ages thoroughly entertained.
Makadi Water World (Aquapark Makadi Bay):
This is a huge highlight for families! As one of the largest aqua parks in the region, it boasts an incredible 50 different slides, wave pools, and water play areas suitable for toddlers, teens, and those who are still kids at heart.
Dolphin World Show:
A short trip away, this popular attraction features interactive and entertaining shows with dolphins, sea lions, and walruses—a guaranteed crowd-pleaser for the whole family.
Mini Egypt Park:
For a little educational fun, this park near Hurghada (a simple day trip) offers miniature versions of Egypt’s most famous landmarks, letting you see the Pyramids, Sphinx, and Luxor Temple all in one place.
Desert Atmosphere & Adventure Excursions at Makadi Red Sea
Safari Makadi
Step away from the Makadi Bay beach for a moment and explore the dramatic desert atmosphere that frames the resort town. A desert adventure is one of the most exciting Makadi Bay things to do.
Quad Biking Safari
Hop on an ATV or quad bike and roar through the Eastern Desert’s golden sands. You can choose a sunrise, daytime, or thrilling sunset tour, often including a stop at a traditional Bedouin camp for tea and a taste of local life.
Camel or Horseback Riding
Experience ancient desert travel with a peaceful camel or horseback ride. A sunset ride is particularly magical, with the sky’s colors reflecting over the sands.
Desert Stargazing
The lack of light pollution in the desert makes for some of the best stargazing you’ll ever see. Many tours include professional telescopes and astronomy guides to point out constellations and planets, a truly humbling experience.
History and Culture: Day Tours from Makadi Bay
While Makadi Bay is about relaxation and the Red Sea, its location offers incredible access to Egypt’s ancient wonders, transforming your Hurghada to Makadi Bay trip into a truly holistic Egyptian holiday.
The Land of the Pharaohs: Luxor Excursion
For the history buff, a day trip to Luxor is a must. It’s a long day, but utterly worth it to see the magnificence of ancient Thebes.
Valley of the Kings:
Explore the tombs of Pharaohs, including the famous King Tutankhamun.
Karnak Temple:
Wander through one of the largest religious complexes in the world, marveling at the colossal pillars and statues.
Temple of Hatshepsut and the Colossi of Memnon:
Visit the striking mortuary temple carved into the cliffs and see the giant guardian statues.
Local Flavour: Hurghada to Makadi Bay Day Tours
Hurghada City is the nearest major town and is easily accessible for an afternoon or evening excursion.
Hurghada Marina:
Take a pleasant stroll, enjoy the modern architecture, and dine at one of the many excellent Makadi Bay restaurants or cafes overlooking the yachts.
Old Town (Dahar):
Experience a taste of local life in the traditional markets. This is your spot for authentic local culture and a little Makadi Bay shopping for spices and souvenirs.
Where to Stay: Hotels in Makadi Bay
Sunrise Royal Makadi
Makadi Bay is essentially a resort community known as Madinat Makadi, specifically designed for tourism.
The hotels in Makadi Bay are mostly five-star, all-inclusive, and self-contained, offering everything you need on-site.
Jaz Makadi Spa (Adults Only):
If you’re seeking pure, unadulterated tranquility, the Jaz Makadi Spa in Hurghada is your perfect retreat. It’s an adult-only luxury resort focused on wellness, relaxation, and a peaceful atmosphere.
Sunrise Royal Makadi:
A large, lively, family-friendly option that is highly rated for its service, facilities, and direct access to a great section of the Makadi Bay beach.
Royal Pharaohs Makadi (Style):
Many resorts, such as those with names like Sunrise or Jaz, offer a ‘Royal’ or ‘Palace’ theme. These often provide a blend of luxury, Moorish architecture, and direct beach/reef access. Focusing on a premium all-inclusive experience that makes you feel like Egyptian royalty!
Fort Arabesque Resort, Spa & Villas:
Excellent for diving and snorkeling due to its long, protected beachfront and close proximity to the house reef.
The hotels in Makadi Bay are mostly five-star, all-inclusive, and self-contained, offering everything you need on-site.
However, if you’ve fallen completely in love with the area and are looking for a permanent seaside residence or investment opportunity, explore Makadi Heights by Orascom for beautiful homes and apartments with stunning sea views.
Check Makadi Heights Beach Houses
The Verdict: Why Makadi Bay is a Must-Visit
From the moment you step onto the soft Makadi Bay beach and gaze at the dazzling Red Sea, you’ll understand the magic.
It perfectly blends a tranquil resort atmosphere with thrilling adventures.
You can spend your morning deep into Makadi Bay scuba diving, your afternoon zooming across the desert on a quad bike. And you’re enjoying a quiet, gourmet meal at your luxury hotel.
It’s the ultimate Egyptian holiday, focusing on the breathtaking beauty of the Red Sea and incredible value.
Why Makadi Bay is – Maybe – Your Next Home?
Here’s a little secret many travelers discover
: once you visit Makadi Bay, it’s incredibly hard to leave. The lifestyle, the sunshine, and the proximity to the crystal clear waters begin to feel like home!
If the thought of waking up every day to this paradise sounds like your ultimate life goal, then perhaps it’s time to move from booking a holiday to securing your future. Why limit yourself to a week when you can live here forever?

Nawy’s website
, and one of our property consultants will call you right away.
Key FAQ: How far from Makadi Bay is Cairo?
Cairo is approximately
430 to 500 kilometers (270 to 310 miles)
from Makadi, depending on the route.
Travel Time by Road (Bus/Private Transfer)
The drive usually takes between 5.5 and 8 hours, depending on traffic, stops, and the vehicle type. It’s a very long day for a round-trip, so an overnight stay in Cairo is highly recommended if you choose to go by road.
Travel Time by Air (Most Convenient)
The best option is to take a quick flight from Hurghada International Airport (HRG). It is close to Makadi, to Cairo International Airport (CAI). The flight itself is only about 1 to 1.5 hours. That’s making this the most comfortable and time-efficient way to visit the capital.

Il Monte Galala

R5 New Capital: Top Compounds, Amenities & Investment Guide

--- CONTENT ---
R5 New Capital
If you have been hearing a lot about R5 New Capital and wondering why everyone is talking about it, you are in the right place.
R5 is one of the most charming and well-planned residential districts in New Administrative Capital. It blends modern life with a classic touch inspired by Paris and Khedivial Cairo.
Yes, it really feels like you are living inside a beautifully designed city within a city. People are considering investing in this upcoming area because it is calm, green, organized, and filled with everything you need.
If you want a home where comfort, wise investment, and practicality meet, this guide will walk you through it all in a simple and friendly way.
Let’s dive in.
R5 New Capital: Overview
What truly sets the New Capital R5 District apart is its unique and luxurious architectural style.
The developers masterfully broke away from monotonous, stereotypical designs, opting instead to mimic the distinct urban vernacular of Paris and Khedivial Cairo. Spanning a massive total area of approximately
984.67 feddans
(nearly 1,000 acres), its placement is key to its appeal.
This design philosophy has earned it the nickname “New Garden City.”
The district’s immense area is thoughtfully divided into four distinct neighborhoods, each with its own character:
19th Century Western Architecture:
Featuring classic, timeless designs reminiscent of old-world European cities.
Modern Architecture:
Showcasing contemporary, sleek, and innovative designs.
Villas:
A dedicated area for luxurious standalone, twin, and townhouses, offering privacy and spacious living.
Towers:
Predominantly earmarked for commercial, administrative, and mixed-use purposes, including stunning residential towers with hotel units.
The residential units across R5 are diverse, encompassing 59 prototypes of residential buildings (often with a commercial ground floor), alongside four prototypes of villas and townhouse units. This variety ensures there is a perfect home for every taste and need.
Where is R5 New Capital Located?
R5 New Capital District boasts a strategic and highly desirable location within  New Administrative Capital:
Strategic Positioning:
R5 is situated in the heart of New Capital City, with easy access to vital main axes like the Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed South Axis and the Central Axis.
Proximity to Key Landmarks:
It’s located near the Financial and Business District, the Iconic Tower, the Diplomatic District, and the Government District, making it an ideal hub for professionals and families alike.
A River View:
Perhaps the most spectacular feature is its adjoining position to the iconic Green River, the central park of New Capital that is set to be the largest in the world.
This proximity offers residents stunning views and direct access to expansive green spaces for recreation and tranquility.
The exceptional location makes R5 a highly convenient and prestigious address, solidifying its status as one of the top residential districts in the entire New Administrative Capital.
Speaking of the New Capital map and its amazing districts, take a second to read this blog:
R7 or R8 at New Capital? Check Compounds, Location & Photos
What Amenities Does R5 New Capital Offer?
Life in the residential district R5 is designed for utmost comfort, convenience, and luxury.
The project’s comprehensive master plan includes a full spectrum of world-class facilities and ancillary buildings, ensuring residents have everything they need right within their community.
Leisure and Lifestyle Amenities In R5 in New Capital City
R5 New Capital allocates a significant portion of its land to lush landscapes and open areas, promoting a healthy and vibrant outdoor lifestyle:
Green River Access:
Direct connection to the massive central park and garden city concept.
Sports Clubs:
State-of-the-art facilities for a variety of sports and fitness activities.
Open Spaces:
Expansive parks, walking tracks, and communal areas for relaxation and socializing.
Kids Area:
Dedicated, safe, and engaging play areas for children.
Essential and Ancillary Services in R5 in New Capital City
R5 in New Capital City
The district is fully integrated and self-sufficient, featuring a host of essential facilities:
Healthcare:
A complete hospital and a family health clinic.
Education:
Multiple schools to provide high-quality education for all age groups.
Security & Administration:
A police station, civil defense center, and post office.
Hospitality:
Two major hotels are strategically located in the Tower Neighborhood, near the Green River.
Infrastructure:
Every square plot shares a common basement dedicated entirely to secure, underground parking spaces.
The integration of all these services, from schools to hospitals to a dedicated
kids’ area
, within the district underscores the commitment to creating a full-service, secure, and thriving community in the R5 New Capital District.
Compounds in R5 – Top Choices
R5 in New Capital City is home to several high-end projects developed by leading real estate names, further enhancing its luxury profile.
These compounds offer various residential units and specialized experiences, all benefiting from the district’s unparalleled location and amenities.
Mayadin New Capital
Mayadin New Capital
is a luxurious development offering a high-end collection of residential units, including apartments, standalone villas, twin houses, and townhouses.
Mayadin New Capital Compound
It boasts well landscaping, a social clubhouse, a top-notch gym, and a variety of commercial and dining venues. The developer of Mayadin New Capital is
Saudi Egyptian Developers (SED)
.
New Garden City Compound
New Garden City Compound
New Garden City
is a high-end community brought to life by the experts at City Edge Developments. Situated right in the lively center of New Capital, this is arguably the most desirable area in eastern Cairo for those who crave a modern, sophisticated lifestyle.
Best of all?
You’ll find a wide variety of homes for sale here, from different sizes to price points, making it easy to discover the perfect fit for your new life.
Baroque Compound
Experience vitality and elegance at Baroque Compound in New Administrative Capital.
Nestled in the highly sought-after Residential District 5, this compound boasts a truly ideal location next to the beautiful Green River.
Getting in and out is a breeze; residents enjoy perfect ease of access, making daily life effortless.
Why Invest in R5 New Capital?
Choosing the R5 New Capital District is an investment in a future of luxury, convenience, and stability.
And if you ask
why invest in New Capital
, especially R5, it’s easy to answer:
High Investment Return:
The prime location adjacent to the Central Business District (CBD) and the Green River, combined with its unique architectural vision, ensures continuous appreciation in property value.
Unmatched Quality of Life:
The total area is efficiently used to maximize green spaces (up to 80% in some parts) and offer an integrated lifestyle with all services within reach.
Architectural Uniqueness:
The “New Garden City” aesthetic stands out. It’s offering a distinguished living experience compared to other developments.
Integrated Infrastructure:
Modern and fully developed infrastructure. That’s including robust communications, security systems, electrical, HVAC, and road networks. This guarantees a seamless living environment.
New Capital Top Compounds

Things to do in Sharm El Sheikh
Have you been planning on spending your vacation in Sharm El Sheikh, yet you are still uncertain about the activities you can enjoy in this mesmerising earthly heaven? No worries, this blog article is all about the top and most fun things to do in Sharm El Sheikh.
This full guide will be your number one planner for your trip. You will get to know the top activities you can practice, places to visit, top beaches, and hotels to stay in Sharm El Sheikh.
All you need to do now is sit back, stay focused, and get ready to plan for your journey after reading this 15-minute blog.
Overview of the City
Activities and Things to Do
Places to Visit
Beaches to Enjoy
World-class Hotels
Sharm El Sheikh: City of Magic
We cannot miss giving you an overview of the city before proceeding to talk about the top things to do in Sharm El Sheikh. This magical city’s development started in 1968, and it was intended to be one of the most stunning coastal spots by the Red Sea.
The city is part of South Sinai Governorate and is located on the Aqaba Gulf, in the southeastern part of the Sinai Peninsula, at the intersection of the Aqaba and Suez Gulfs.
Sharm El Shiekh is also a host of many:
Enjoyable activities and things to do
Fun places to visit
Stunning beaches
World-class hotels
Let us, in each of the following sections, talk about each of these items in a bit of detail so you can thoroughly plan for your trip.
Enjoyable Activities & Things To Do in Sharm El Sheikh
In fact, the city of beauty has many awesome and fun activities to do. Activities and things to do in Sharm El Sheikh can be classified into underwater activities and surface activities.
Since the city is well-known for its pristine waters, a lot of activities are allocated to the beach and the water.
Moreover, activities and things to do in Sharm El Sheikh vary between shopping, dining, sightseeing, relaxing, and enjoying family quality time. All in all, the city offers an inclusive, fun experience for the whole family.
Things to do in Sharm El Sheikh
Underwater Activities: Wonders of the Underwater World
Being one of the major coastal hotspots on the Red Sea, enjoying crystal clear waters, and a unique shoreline, Sharm El Sheikh is home to many underwater activities, such as:
1. Snorkeling, diving, and scuba diving
Snorkling destinations
include Ras Mohammed National Park, Tiran Island, Shark’s Reef, Yolanda Reef, Sharm El Maya, Middle Garden, Temple Reef, and Far Garden Reef.
Diving & Scuba diving destinations and centers
include SS Thistlegorm Wreck, Jackson Reef, Thomas Reef, Gordon Reef, Woodhouse Reef, Ras Ghozlani, Shark Observatory, Red Sea Diving College, Camel Dive Club, Sinai Divers)
Know More Snorkeling & Diving Spots

3. Going on boat tours and cruises

Dolphina Park

Sharm El Sheikh Mesmerizing Beaches
Sharm El Sheikh is home to many pristine, stunning beaches, on which various types of water activities can be practiced. One can enjoy various water activities such as diving or snorkeling, also kitesurfing is a good option.
Moreover, you can just sit and relax by the crystal clear waters and white soft sands. To be more precise, Sharm El Sheikh hosts a wide selection of public and private bays and beaches, which include but are not limited to the following:
Naama Bay Beach (most famous, awesome for water sports)
Sharks Bay (the calmest beach, hosts many diving & snorkeling centers)
Nabq Bay (better known for kitesurfing thanks to its calm, windy weather)
Ras Um Sid Beach (best known for its awesome coral life)
Ras Nasrani Beach (great spot for watching diverse fish types)
El Fanar Beach (a great spot for watching shoreline
marine life
)
Terrazzina Beach (the perfect spot for a family weekend trip)
Sharm El Maya Bay (found in the old town, kid-friendly thanks to shallow waters)
Farsha Beach (enjoys a unique hillside view of the beach with many cafes around)
White Island (crystal clear waters and white soft sands)
White King Beach (usually a spot for professional divers)
Palm Beach (a public, calm beach)
Beaches of the city

An overview of the city (one of the coastal spots by the Aqaba Gulf, Red Sea)

The premium wildlife activities (safari, camping, and hiking)

FAQs
Where is Sharm El Sheikh?
Sharm El Sheikh is located on the Gulf of Aqaba in the southeastern part of the Sinai Peninsula.
Where to stay in Sharm El Sheikh, Egypt?
You can choose among 100+ luxury hotels to stay, such as Rixos Hotels, Four Seasons Hotel, and Hilton Sharm El Sheikh.
What to do in Sharm El Sheikh, Egypt?
You can enjoy a wide variety of activities, such as water activities (diving and snorkeling), desert and wildlife adventures (safari and camping), shopping, and enjoying nightlife (from the old market and Soho Square).
How to get from Cairo to Sharm El Sheikh?
You can go from Cairo to Sharm El Sheikh by car in approximately 6 hours, by bus in almost 8 hours, or by plane in almost 1.5 hours.
What is the best time of the year to visit Sharm El Sheikh?
You can visit the city all year round; however, it is recommended to visit it during spring and autumn to enjoy its warm weather and fewer crowds.

Golf Villas Egypt: Top Golf Communities in Cairo, Sahel & More

--- CONTENT ---
Golf Villas
Golf communities in Egypt have moved from being a niche option to a mainstream choice for buyers who want something different from typical residential compounds. They offer more space, more greenery, and a noticeably calmer environment. Instead of dense buildings and crowded streets, these developments are built around golf courses, which shape the entire lifestyle.
Today, golf villas are available in several key locations across Egypt. Including East Cairo, West Cairo, Ain Sokhna, El Gouna, and North Coast.
Some buyers choose them as primary homes, others as vacation properties, and many see them as a long-term investment with strong demand.
What Makes Golf Villas So Special?
In recent years, real estate seekers in Egypt have increasingly searched for villas for sale and luxury villas that offer golf course views, privacy, and secure community living. That’s where golf villas come in, blending high‑end design with leisure, recreation, and investment appeal.
Here is why golf villas continue to grow in demand across Egypt:
Vast green spaces and open landscapes
Premium privacy and quiet surroundings
Strong resale and rental demand
Access to elite clubhouses and leisure facilities
Ideal environment for families, professionals, and retirees
Active & Social Lifestyle
Let’s explore them areas by area so you can find the perfect fit.
Golf Communities in East Cairo
East Cairo has emerged as a hotspot for high-end residential developments. If you’re looking for a home with breathtaking golf course views, East Cairo offers a variety of options that cater to different tastes and budgets.
From expansive private villas with gardens and terraces to modern townhouse-style homes within gated communities, there’s something for everyone.
If you’re looking for golf villas with great views and easy access to the city, this is where to start.
1. Suli Golf Residence in New Capital City
Suli Golf Residence
If you’re looking for a serene yet vibrant community in the heart of New Capital,
Suli Golf Residence
is definitely worth checking out.
This exceptional gated community is designed with livability in mind, combining modern architecture, lush green spaces, and user-friendly facilities that make day-to-day life a breeze.
2. West ElGolf in New Cairo
Next up is West Golf Compound, one of the most distinguished luxury communities in New Cairo. Known for its lavish homes with contemporary designs, the compound caters to modern families who value both elegance and practicality.
Living here means enjoying a wide range of amenities that make everyday life comfortable and convenient. From top-notch security to recreational facilities,
West Golf Katameya
has it all.
Beyond the homes themselves, the compound is like a natural sanctuary, with green spaces and serene surroundings where residents can relax and enjoy the outdoors.
3. Golf Vistas Uptown Cairo
Golf Residence Compound
For those who love panoramic city views, Golf Vistas in
Uptown Cairo
is a standout option.
Situated 200 meters above sea level, this prestigious community offers breathtaking vistas of Cairo’s skyline and the lush golf courses below.
Golf Vistas blends urban sophistication with serene living.
That’s offering a luxurious collection of properties that cater to a variety of lifestyles.
4. Mirage City Golf Club
Mirage Golf
If you’re drawn to contemporary design and resort-style living,
Mirage City Golf Club
is an excellent choice.
This community combines sleek architecture with expansive open spaces, creating a sense of elegance and comfort.
Mirage City is ideal for families seeking a balanced lifestyle: kids can enjoy safe play areas and recreational facilities, while adults can take advantage of fitness centers, clubhouses, and golf courses.
The atmosphere strikes a perfect mix of exclusivity and social living, allowing residents to feel part of a close-knit community while enjoying modern comforts.
Whether it’s a modern villa for your family or an elegant retreat in the capital, residents here enjoy exclusivity, comfort, and some of the best scenic views in East Cairo.
Golf Living in West Cairo
If East Cairo is about accessibility and modern convenience, West Cairo is all about prestige, luxury, and expansive living. This area is home to some of the most distinguished golf residence compounds in Egypt, making it ideal for buyers searching for homes for sale that combine elegance, comfort, and an active lifestyle.

is a shining example of high-end living in Egypt.

Life here is all about convenience and leisure. From expansive clubhouses and fitness centers to beautifully landscaped gardens and recreational areas, every corner of Golf Solimania in 6th October is designed to make daily living a pleasure. Whether you want to relax, socialize, or enjoy outdoor activities, this community has it all.

After exploring the vibrant golf communities of Cairo, sometimes you just want to leave the city behind and escape to the beach.
Ain Sokhna offers the perfect combination of seaside tranquility and luxury golf living.
Imagine waking up in your villa, stepping out onto a green golf course, and enjoying the sparkling waters of the Gulf of Suez just minutes away.
It’s the ideal blend of relaxation, recreation, and elegance, a true getaway without leaving the country.
Jaz Little Venice Golf Resort
Golf Villas

Here, every day feels like a vacation.

With beautifully designed homes, green surroundings, and top-tier amenities, it’s a place where families can unwind, entertain guests, or simply enjoy the serenity of coastal living.

Its strategic location in Al Alamein ensures that residents can enjoy both privacy and accessibility, being close to other luxury resorts and gated communities.
At Golf Porto Marina, you’re not just buying a villa, you’re embracing a lifestyle of tranquility, luxury, and scenic coastal living.

Family Park El Rehab Cairo: Its Tickets, Activities & Opening Hours
===================================================================

--- CONTENT ---
Family park cairo el rehab
Bored of the same cafés, malls, and crowded weekend plans? If you’re looking for a place where kids can run freely, parents can actually relax, and everyone leaves in a good mood, Family Park Cairo is the answer.
Think wide green spaces, kid-friendly activities, chill picnic vibes, and enough options to keep everyone happy without breaking the bank.
Located in Al Rehab, right in the heart of New Cairo, Family Park Egypt has become a favorite escape for families who want to unplug, relax, and enjoy quality time outdoors.
So, if you are planning a casual day out, celebrating birthdays, or just want fresh air away from traffic, this guide covers everything you need to know about Family Park in Al Rehab.
Family Park Cairo
Before getting into the details, it helps to understand why so many families keep coming back to Family Park New Cairo.
The park is designed with families in mind. It is spacious, organized, and offers a mix of entertainment and relaxation. Kids are free to explore and play, while parents enjoy the peace of open areas, shaded seating, and walking paths. It feels safe, welcoming, and far from the usual city noise.
That balance is what makes Family Park Cairo stand out.
Family Park Location
The Family Park location is one of its biggest advantages.
The park is located inside Al Rehab City, just off
Suez Road
, making it easy to reach from most areas in Cairo.
If you are coming from
New Cairo
, Nasr City, Heliopolis, or even Downtown, the drive is straightforward.
Many visitors also use ride-hailing apps, and parking is available nearby, which makes the experience stress-free.
Because of its prime location, Family Park Cairo is often included in weekend plans for families living in
El Rehab
and the surrounding areas.
Family Park El Rehab Opening Hours
Now that you know how to get there, let’s talk timing.
The family park opening hours are from
9:00 AM to 10:00 PM
Family Park opening hours usually start in the morning and extend into the evening, giving visitors flexibility depending on their plans. Mornings are perfect if you want a quiet experience, especially for picnics and walking.
Late afternoons and evenings are ideal during warmer months, as the weather becomes more comfortable.
Family Park Tickets and Entrance Fees
Family Park Cairo​
Once you plan your visit, the next thing to consider is Family Park tickets.
The
Family Park ticket price
is budget-friendly compared to many entertainment venues in Cairo. The entrance fees are usually per person, with discounts sometimes available for children. This makes it easy for large families to enjoy a full day out without worrying about high costs.
General Family Park El Rehab Fees:
Weekdays (Sunday – Wednesday)
:150 EGP per person.
Weekends (Thursday – Friday)
: 250 EGP per person (higher demand days).
Children Under 4:
Free entry
Some activities inside the park may require separate tickets, but general entry gives you access to green spaces, picnic areas, and walking paths.
Important Disclaimer
Ticket prices, entrance fees, and activity costs may change at any time depending on seasons, holidays, or park management decisions. To avoid surprises, it is always best to check the official ticket counters.
Activities to Enjoy at Family Park Cairo
Once you step inside, this is where the real fun begins.
Family Park El Rehab offers a wide variety of entertainment and cultural activities, making it more than just a green space.
Entertainment Activities
Family Park​ Cairo
Kids and adults alike can enjoy several exciting attractions, including a small train ride that loops through the park and adds a playful touch to the experience. There is also the famous Magic River, which gives families a relaxing ride with a fun twist.
The entertainment village is a favorite for children, offering interactive spaces and games, while the open playgrounds allow kids to run freely and burn off energy. For animal lovers, the zoo and safari area adds an educational and entertaining element to the visit.
Cultural and Educational Activities
Family Park Egypt is not only about fun. It also includes cultural centers, theaters, and even a historical museum that introduces children and adults to different educational experiences in a simple and engaging way.
There is also a 3D cinema, which is a great option if you want to mix outdoor activities with something a little different.
Picnic Areas and Relaxing Spaces
After all that activity, you will definitely want a place to sit and relax.
One of the strongest features of
Family Park Cairo
is its well-designed picnic areas. Families often bring their own food, mats, and games, turning the visit into a full-day outdoor experience. The green lawns and shaded spots make it easy to relax without feeling crowded.
These picnic areas are also a popular choice for small gatherings and casual celebrations.
Family Park El Rehab Activities
Restaurants and Food Options Inside Family Park
If you prefer to eat on-site, you have plenty of options.
Family Park New Cairo includes a variety of restaurants and cafés to suit different tastes. There is a restaurant complex that offers multiple choices, along with themed dining spots like
Forest Restaurant
and
Lake Restaurant
. For lighter options,
Rendezvous Cafeteria
is a popular stop for drinks and snacks.
This variety means you can comfortably spend hours inside the park without needing to leave for food.
You can also check
9+ Must-Visit New Cairo Restaurants For Every Taste
for more dining venues
Celebrating Birthdays at Family Park El Rehab
Family park
Planning birthdays becomes much easier at Family Park El Rehab.
The open spaces allow children to play freely, while parents enjoy a stress-free environment. Many families choose to celebrate birthdays here because it feels natural and flexible, without strict time limits or crowded indoor settings.
With entertainment options, food nearby, and plenty of space, birthdays at Family Park Cairo often turn into memorable full-day events.
Final Thoughts: Why Family Park Cairo Is Always a Good Idea
At the end of the day, Family Park Cairo is one of those places that simply works for everyone. It gives families the space to relax, kids the freedom to play, and adults a break from the usual city rush. With its easy location in Al Rehab, affordable ticket prices, diverse activities, and welcoming atmosphere, it continues to be a favorite spot for families across Cairo.
And if spending time at Family Park has you thinking about a lifestyle that offers more green spaces, family-friendly communities, and everyday comfort, Nawy can help you take the next step.
Explore homes in Al Rehab and New Cairo through Nawy, and let one of our property consultants guide you toward a place where quality family time is part of your daily life, not just a weekend plan.

Another high-end development by TMG, Al Rabwa Compound, has one of the best golf courses in West Cairo. It extends over an expansive area of 2,400 SQM, covered in verdant landscapes and sparkling lakes. The project is located 800 meters from the Cairo – Alexandria Desert Road.
Working Hours
: Contact the club directly to book
Contact Details
: (202) 38521706
The Allegria Golf Club House
Golf Courses in Hurghada
There are many
fun things to do in Hurghada, Egypt
, one of which is having a nice game with your loved ones in the city’s golf clubs.
Some of Hurghada’s golf courses and clubs are:
Steigenberger Golf Course in El Gouna
Situated in El Gouna, Steigenberger Golf Resort has an 18-hole championship golf course with breathtaking views of the surrounding greenery. It is a mere 30 minutes from Hurghada International Airport.
Working Hours
: varying hours depending on the season (winter/summer)
2.
Ancient Sands Golf Club
One of the best golf courses in El Gouna, Hurghada, is the Ancient Sands Golf Club. It is an 18-hole championship course, extending over an impressive 6,788 SQM. It is also suitable for all levels. Additionally, it features 3-hole golf academy with a full practice facility.
Working Hours
: 7 AM till sunset
Contact Details
: golf@ancientsandsresort.com (for tee times and bookings)
3.
Madinet Makadi Golf Resort
A prominent golf destination, Madinet Makadi Golf Resort has an 18-hole championship course. It was designed by the renowned European Golf Design. It is also around 30 minutes from Hurghada Airport.
Working Hours
: 7 AM till sunset
4.
Somabay Golf Courses
Soma Bay
, Egypt, has a marvellous 18-hole Championship Gary Player Signature Golf Course. It is known for its challenging, state-of-the-art design, in addition to its proximity to the Red Sea coastline.
Working Hours
: 9 AM – 9 PM
The Cascades 18-hole championship course isn’t the only one in Somabay, Hurghada. Moreover, there is The Par 3 Challenge Golf Course, which was designed by Tim Lobb and James Edwards. & it is suitable for all levels!
Madinat Makadi Golf

Sokhna Golf Club Course
The Sokhna Golf Club has an outstanding 27-hole, par-27 championship golf course. It is a mere hour’s drive from Cairo, so it serves as a flawless coastal and golfing gateway from the hustle and bustle of the city.

El Sahel – North Coast is one of the mesmerizing summer destinations in Egypt. Looking forward to blending summertime relaxation with golfing?
North Coast features many upscale golf courses, including but not limited to the following:
Marassi Golf Course
Marassi Sidi Abdel Rahman Resort
features an 18-hole, par-72 championship golf course, designed by Peter Harradine. Being a fully-fledged coastal community, you will enjoy a luxurious golfing experience. It also hosts modern facilities, such as a clubhouse, pro shop, and academy, all within Address Marassi.
This expansive golf course features vibrant greenery and water bodies. It’s worth noting that the resort is located on Kilo 126 of the International Coastal Road.

Just put your game face on, unwind, and enjoy a marvellous time!
FAQs
1. How long is an 18-hole golf course?
A standard 18-hole golf course is about 6,600 yards. A typical game would be approximately 4 hours.
2. How many golf courses in Egypt?
Around 20 – 25 golf courses.
3. Are there golf courses in Egypt next to the Pyramids?
Yes, including but not limited to Dreamland Golf Resort and Mena House Golf Club.

Check out Day Use Spots in Cairo
Things to Do & Places to Go in Zamalek
Zamalek is one of the hidden gems in Cairo, Egypt. In fact, it has many exceptional things to do and places to go. Planning for a day there will give the best of both worlds: enjoy a fun outing, and have fine dining.
Places and activities in Zamalek include, but are not limited to:
Taking a flouka, boat, or Nile bus on the Nile
Visiting museums and historical sites such as Aisha Fahmy Palace, The Aquarium Grotto Garden, Greater Cairo Public Library, Museum of Islamic Ceramics, and Mahmoud Mokhtar Museum
Attending a concert or a cultural event in the Cairo Opera House, Zamalek, and El Sawy Culture Wheel
Watching movies in the vintage cinemas, such as Zamalek Cinema
Shopping in boutiques, antique shops, and galleries
Take a walk by the Corniche
Reading and going book shopping in one of the well-known bookstores, such as Diwan
Don’t forget that you can enjoy an extended stay at one of the lavish hotels by the Nile. Those include Cairo Marriott Hotel & Omar Khayyam Casino, Hotel Sofitel Cairo Nile El-Gezira, Hilton Cairo Zamalek Residences, and Hotel Novotel Cairo El Borg.
So, knowing about the best restaurants in Zamalek, the top places to visit, and things to do, let’s tell you what you should do now!
Wrapping up & Taking Action!
In this article, we have:
Listed top, well-known restaurants in Zamalek, such as Le Pacha 1901 and Luuma
Added another list of bakeries and breakfast places in Zamalek
Talked about the various places and activities you can do in the area (cultural, historical, shopping)
Now, don’t forget to check out the huge property listings available on Nawy’s website and pick the property you would love to call “home”.
